Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, kũu Dameski nĩ kwarĩ na mũrutwo wetagwo Anania. Mwathani akĩmwarĩria na kĩoneki, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Anania!” Nake agĩcookia atĩrĩ, “Niĩ ũyũ Mwathani.”
English: Now there was a certain disciple at Damascus named Ananias. The Lord said to him in a vision, “Ananias!” He said, “Behold, it’s me, Lord.”

Kikuyu: Nake Mwathani akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Thiĩ kwa Judasi o kũu njĩra ĩrĩa ĩtagwo Nũngarũ, ũrie mũndũ uumĩte Tariso wĩtagwo Saũlũ, nĩ ũndũ nĩkũhooya arahooya.
English: The Lord said to him, “Arise and go to the street which is called Straight, and inquire in the house of Judah§ for one named Saul, a man of Tarsus. For behold, he is praying,

Kikuyu: Nĩonete mũndũ kĩoneki-inĩ wĩtagwo Anania agĩũka kũmũigĩrĩra moko, nĩguo acooke kuona.”
English: and in a vision he has seen a man named Ananias coming in and laying his hands on him, that he might receive his sight.”

Kikuyu: Anania akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Mwathani, nĩnjiguĩte ũhoro mũingĩ ũkoniĩ mũndũ ũcio, na maũndũ maingĩ mooru marĩa aaneka andũ aku arĩa aamũre kũũrĩa Jerusalemu.
English: But Ananias answered, “Lord, I have heard from many about this man, how much evil he did to your saints at Jerusalem.

Kikuyu: Na okĩte gũkũ arĩ na wathani kuuma kũrĩ athĩnjĩri-Ngai arĩa anene, nĩgeetha anyiite andũ arĩa othe makayagĩra rĩĩtwa rĩaku.”
English: Here he has authority from the chief priests to bind all who call on your name.”

Kikuyu: No Mwathani akĩĩra Anania atĩrĩ, “Thiĩ, tondũ ũndũ ũcio nĩ kĩndũ gĩakwa kĩrĩa ndĩĩthuurĩire gĩa gũtwara ũhoro wa rĩĩtwa rĩakwa kũrĩ andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ, na athamaki, o na kũrĩ andũ a Isiraeli.
English: But the Lord said to him, “Go your way, for he is my chosen vessel to bear my name before the nations and kings, and the children of Israel.

Kikuyu: Nĩngũmuonia ũrĩa arĩ o nginya anyamaarĩke nĩ ũndũ wa rĩĩtwa rĩakwa.”
English: For I will show him how many things he must suffer for my name’s sake.”

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo Anania agĩthiĩ na agĩtoonya nyũmba ĩyo. Akĩigĩrĩra Saũlũ moko, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Saũlũ, mũrũ wa Ithe witũ, Mwathani, o we Jesũ ũrĩa ũrakuumĩrĩire ũrĩ njĩra-inĩ ũgĩũka gũkũ, nĩandũmĩte nĩguo ũcooke kuona, na ũiyũrio na Roho Mũtheru.”
English: Ananias departed and entered into the house. Laying his hands on him, he said, “Brother Saul, the Lord, who appeared to you on the road by which you came, has sent me that you may receive your sight and be filled with the Holy Spirit.”

Kikuyu: O hĩndĩ ĩyo, tũnyamũ twahaanaga ta ngaracũ tũkiuma maitho-inĩ ma Saũlũ, tũkĩgũa thĩ, nake akĩhota kuona rĩngĩ. Agĩũkĩra na akĩbatithio,
English: Immediately something like scales fell from his eyes, and he received his sight. He arose and was baptized.

Kikuyu: na aarĩkia kũrĩa irio, akĩgĩa na hinya. Saũlũ agĩikara mĩthenya ĩigana ũna marĩ hamwe na arutwo kũu Dameski.
English: He took food and was strengthened. Saul stayed several days with the disciples who were at Damascus.

Kikuyu: Na hĩndĩ ĩyo akĩambĩrĩria kũhunjia thunagogi-inĩ atĩ Jesũ nĩ Mũrũ wa Ngai.
English: Immediately in the synagogues he proclaimed the Christ, that he is the Son of God.

Kikuyu: Andũ othe arĩa maamũiguire makĩgega makĩũria atĩrĩ, “Githĩ mũndũ ũyũ tiwe ũraagithĩtie andũ thayũ kũu Jerusalemu gatagatĩ- inĩ ka arĩa makayagĩra rĩĩtwa rĩu? Na githĩ ndokĩte gũkũ nĩgeetha amanyiite amatware marĩ ohe kũrĩ athĩnjĩri-Ngai arĩa anene?”
English: All who heard him were amazed, and said, “Isn’t this he who in Jerusalem made havoc of those who called on this name? And he had come here intending to bring them bound before the chief priests!”

Kikuyu: Nowe Saũlũ agĩkĩrĩrĩria kũgĩa na hinya, na akĩgegia Ayahudi arĩa maatũũrĩte kũu Dameski na ũndũ wa kũmaiguithia na ma atĩ Jesũ nĩwe Kristũ.
English: But Saul increased more in strength, and confounded the Jews who lived at Damascus, proving that this is the Christ.

Kikuyu: Thuutha wa mĩthenya mĩingĩ gũthira, Ayahudi magĩcookania ndundu mamũũrage,
English: When many days were fulfilled, the Jews conspired together to kill him,

Kikuyu: no Saũlũ akĩmenya mũbango ũrĩa maarĩ naguo. Mũthenya na ũtukũ maaikaraga mamuoheirie ihingo-inĩ cia itũũra inene nĩguo mamũũrage.
English: but their plot became known to Saul. They watched the gates both day and night that they might kill him,

Kikuyu: No arũmĩrĩri aake makĩmuoya ũtukũ, makĩmũharũrũkia arĩ gĩkabũ-inĩ mamũgereirie mwanya warĩ rũthingo-inĩ.
English: but his disciples took him by night and let him down through the wall, lowering him in a basket.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa aakinyire Jerusalemu, akĩgeria kwĩgwatania na arutwo, no othe nĩmamwĩtigagĩra, tondũ matietĩkagia atĩ kũna aarĩ mũrutwo.
English: When Saul had come to Jerusalem, he tried to join himself to the disciples; but they were all afraid of him, not believing that he was a disciple.

Kikuyu: No Baranaba akĩmuoya akĩmũtwara kũrĩ atũmwo. Akĩmeera ũrĩa Saũlũ oonire Mwathani arĩ rũgendo-inĩ rwake, na ũrĩa Mwathani aamwarĩirie, na ũrĩa aahunjirie kũu Dameski thĩinĩ wa rĩĩtwa rĩa Jesũ ategwĩtigĩra.
English: But Barnabas took him and brought him to the apostles, and declared to them how he had seen the Lord on the way, and that he had spoken to him, and how at Damascus he had preached boldly in the name of Jesus.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Saũlũ agĩikara na atũmwo, na agaceeraga kũu Jerusalemu, akĩaragia na ũcamba thĩinĩ wa rĩĩtwa rĩa Mwathani, ategwĩtigĩra.
English: He was with them entering into† Jerusalem,

Kikuyu: Ningĩ nĩaaragia na agakararanagia na Ayahudi arĩa maaciarĩirwo kwa Ayunani, no nao makageria ũrĩa mangĩmũũraga.
English: preaching boldly in the name of the Lord Jesus.‡ He spoke and disputed

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ maamenyire ũguo, makĩmuoya, makĩmũikũrũkia nginya Kaisarea, makĩmũtũma Tariso.
English: When the brothers† knew it, they brought him down to Caesarea and sent him off to Tarsus.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo kanitha wa Judea guothe, na Galili, na Samaria ũgĩkenera ihinda rĩa thayũ. Nĩwon- gereirwo hinya; na ũkĩũmĩrĩrio nĩ Roho Mũtheru, na ũkĩongererwo andũ, nao magĩtũũra metigĩrĩte Mwathani.
English: So the assemblies throughout all Judea, Galilee, and Samaria had peace and were built up. They were multiplied, walking in the fear of the Lord and in the comfort of the Holy Spirit.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, rĩrĩa Petero aaceeraga kũndũ na kũndũ bũrũri-inĩ, nĩathiire gũceerera andũ arĩa aamũre a kũu Lida.
English: As Peter went throughout all those parts, he came down also to the saints who lived at Lydda.

Kikuyu: Arĩ kũu agĩkora mũndũ wetagwo Ainea warĩ na mũrimũ wa gũkua ciĩga, na aatũũrĩte akomete gĩtanda-inĩ ihinda rĩa mĩaka ĩnana.
English: There he found a certain man named Aeneas, who had been bedridden for eight years because he was paralyzed.

Kikuyu: Petero akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Ainea, Jesũ Kristũ nĩakũhonia.
English: Peter said to him, “Aeneas, Jesus Christ heals you. Get up and make your bed!” Immediately he arose.

Kikuyu: Andũ othe arĩa maatũũraga Lida na Sharoni makĩmuona na makĩgarũrũkĩra Mwathani.
English: All who lived at Lydda and in Sharon saw him, and they turned to the Lord.

Kikuyu: Kũu Jopa nĩ kwarĩ mũrutwo wetagwo Tabitha (narĩo rĩĩtwa rĩu rĩgĩtaũrwo nĩ kuuga Dorokasi), nake aatũũrĩte ekaga maũndũ mega, na agateithagia athĩĩni.
English: Now there was at Joppa a certain disciple named Tabitha, which when translated means Dorcas.‡ This woman was full of good works and acts of mercy which she did.

Kikuyu: O ihinda-inĩ rĩu nĩarũarire na agĩkua, naguo mwĩrĩ wake ũgĩthambio, ũkĩigwo nyũmba ya igũrũ.*
English: In those days, she became sick and died. When they had washed her, they laid her in an upper room.

Kikuyu: Lida kwarĩ hakuhĩ na Jopa; nĩ ũndũ ũcio rĩrĩa arutwo maaiguire atĩ Petero aarĩ kũu Lida, magĩtũma andũ eerĩ makamũringĩrĩrie mamwĩre atĩrĩ, “Twagũthaitha ũka narua!”
English: As Lydda was near Joppa, the disciples, hearing that Peter was there, sent two men§ to him, imploring him not to delay in coming to them.

Kikuyu: Nake Petero agĩthiĩ hamwe nao, na aakinya agĩtwarwo nyũmba ya igũrũ. Atumia othe a ndigwa makĩrũgama, makĩmũthiũrũrũkĩria makĩrĩraga, na makĩmuonagia kanjũ na nguo ingĩ iria Dorokasi aatumĩte rĩrĩa aarĩ nao.
English: Peter got up and went with them. When he had come, they brought him into the upper room. All the widows stood by him weeping, and showing the tunics and other garments which Dorcas had made while she was with them.

Kikuyu: Nake Petero akĩmeera othe moime nja ya nyũmba; agĩcooka agĩturia maru akĩhooya Ngai. Agĩcooka akĩgarũrũkĩra mũtumia ũcio wakuĩte, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Tabitha, ũkĩra,” nake Tabitha akĩhingũra maitho, na oona Petero, agĩĩtiira.
English: Peter sent them all out, and knelt down and prayed. Turning to the body, he said, “Tabitha, get up!” She opened her eyes, and when she saw Peter, she sat up.

Kikuyu: Nake Petero akĩmũnyiita guoko, akĩmũrũgamia. Agĩcooka agĩĩta andũ arĩa meetĩkĩtie, na atumia acio a ndigwa, akĩmũneana kũrĩ o arĩ muoyo.
English: He gave her his hand and raised her up. Calling the saints and widows, he presented her alive.

Kikuyu: Naguo ũhoro ũcio ũkĩmenyeka Jopa guothe, nao andũ aingĩ magĩĩtĩkia Mwathani.
English: This became known throughout all Joppa, and many believed in the Lord.

Kikuyu: Nake Petero agĩikara Jopa mĩthenya mĩingĩ kwa mũndũ wetagwo Simoni, ũrĩa warĩ mũtanduki wa njũũa.†
English: He stayed many days in Joppa with a tanner named Simon.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, kũu Kaisarea nĩ kwarĩ mũndũ wetagwo Korinelio, ũrĩa warĩ mũnene-wa-thigari-igana rĩmwe cia mbũtũ ĩrĩa yoĩkaine ta Mbũtũ ya Italia.
English: Now there was a certain man in Caesarea, Cornelius by name, a centurion of what was called the Italian Regiment,

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũcio na andũ othe a nyũmba yake nĩmeyamũrĩire Ngai na makamwĩtigagĩra; nake nĩaheanaga indo ciake na ũtaana kũrĩ andũ arĩa maarĩ abatari na nĩahooyaga Ngai kaingĩ.
English: a devout man, and one who feared God with all his house, who gave gifts for the needy generously to the people, and always prayed to God.

Kikuyu: Mũthenya ũmwe ta thaa kenda cia mĩaraho-rĩ, akĩona kĩoneki. Akĩona mũraika wa Ngai wega, ũrĩa wokire harĩ we na akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Korinelio!”
English: At about the ninth hour of the day,† he clearly saw in a vision an angel of God coming to him and saying to him, “Cornelius!”

Kikuyu: Korinelio akĩmũrora aiyũrĩtwo nĩ guoya, akĩũria atĩrĩ, “Nĩ kĩĩ Mwathani?” Nake mũraika akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Mahooya maku na indo iria ũheete andũ arĩa athĩĩni nĩikinyĩte igũrũ kũrĩ Ngai rĩrĩ iruta rĩa kũririkanwo.
English: He, fastening his eyes on him and being frightened, said, “What is it, Lord?” He said to him, “Your prayers and your gifts to the needy have gone up for a memorial before God.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, tũma andũ mathiĩ Jopa magĩĩre mũndũ wĩtagwo Simoni, na nowe wĩtagwo Petero.
English: Now send men to Joppa, and get Simon, who is also called Peter.

Kikuyu: Araikara gwa Simoni ũria mũtanduki wa njũũa, o ũrĩa nyũmba yake ĩrĩ rũtere-inĩ rwa iria.”
English: He is staying with a tanner named Simon, whose house is by the seaside.‡

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa mũraika ũcio wamwarĩirie aathiire, Korinelio agĩĩta ndungata ciake igĩrĩ na mũthigari ũmwe weamũrĩire Ngai, na aarĩ ũmwe wa andũ arĩa maamũtungatagĩra.
English: When the angel who spoke to him had departed, Cornelius called two of his household servants and a devout soldier of those who waited on him continually.

Kikuyu: Akĩmeera ũrĩa wothe kwahanĩte agĩcooka akĩmatũma Jopa.
English: Having explained everything to them, he sent them to Joppa.

Kikuyu: Mũthenya ũcio ũngĩ warũmĩrĩire ta thaa thita cia mũthenya marĩ njĩra-inĩ makuhĩrĩirie itũũra rĩu-rĩ, Petero akĩhaica nyũmba-igũrũ nĩgeetha akahooe Ngai.
English: Now on the next day as they were on their journey and got close to the city, Peter went up on the housetop to pray at about noon.

Kikuyu: Nake akĩigua aahũũta na akĩbatario nĩ kĩndũ gĩa kũrĩa, na rĩrĩa irio ciarugagwo-rĩ, akĩgocerera.
English: He became hungry and desired to eat, but while they were preparing, he fell into a trance.

Kikuyu: Nake akĩona igũrũ rĩhingũrĩtwo na kĩndũ gĩatariĩ ta gĩtama kĩnene gĩkĩharũrũkio thĩ kĩnyiitĩtwo mĩthia ĩna.
English: He saw heaven opened and a certain container descending to him, like a great sheet let down by four corners on the earth,

Kikuyu: Nakĩo kĩarĩ na nyamũ cia mĩthemba yothe ya iria irĩ magũrũ mana, o na nyamũ iria itaambaga thĩ, o na nyoni cia rĩera-inĩ.
English: in which were all kinds of four-footed animals of the earth, wild animals, reptiles, and birds of the sky.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo mũgambo ũkĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Petero, ũkĩra. Thĩnja na ũrĩe.”
English: A voice came to him, “Rise, Peter, kill and eat!”

Kikuyu: Petero agĩcookia atĩrĩ, “Aca, Mwathani! Ndirĩ ndarĩa kĩndũ gĩtarĩ kĩamũre, kana kĩndũ kĩrĩ thaahu.”
English: But Peter said, “Not so, Lord; for I have never eaten anything that is common or unclean.”

Kikuyu: Mũgambo ũkĩmwarĩria riita rĩa keerĩ ũkĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Ndũkanoige kĩndũ o na kĩrĩkũ kĩrĩa Ngai atheretie atĩ ti kĩamũre.”
English: A voice came to him again the second time, “What God has cleansed, you must not call unclean.”

Kikuyu: Ũndũ ũyũ wekĩkire maita matatũ, na o rĩmwe gĩtama kĩu gĩgĩcookio na igũrũ.
English: This was done three times, and immediately the thing was received up into heaven.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa Petero eeyũragia gĩtũmi gĩa kĩoneki kĩu oonete-rĩ, andũ arĩa maatũmĩtwo nĩ Korinelio makĩona harĩa nyũmba ya Simoni yarĩ, na makĩrũgama hau kĩhingo-inĩ.
English: Now while Peter was very perplexed in himself what the vision which he had seen might mean, behold, the men who were sent by Cornelius, having made inquiry for Simon’s house, stood before the gate,

Kikuyu: Magĩĩtana, makĩũria kana Simoni ũrĩa wĩtagwo Petero aaikaraga kũu.
English: and called and asked whether Simon, who was also called Peter, was lodging there.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa Petero eeciiragia ũhoro wa kĩoneki kĩu, Roho akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Simoni, harĩ na andũ atatũ maragũcaria.
English: While Peter was pondering the vision, the Spirit said to him, “Behold, three§ men seek you.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, ũkĩra ũikũrũke, ũthiĩ nao na ndũkagĩe na nganja nĩgũkorwo nĩ niĩ ndĩmatũmĩte.”
English: But arise, get down, and go with them, doubting nothing; for I have sent them.”

Kikuyu: Petero agĩikũrũka, akĩĩra andũ acio atĩrĩ, “Nĩ niĩ mũndũ ũcio mũracaria. Mũũkĩte nĩkĩ?”
English: Peter went down to the men, and said, “Behold, I am he whom you seek. Why have you come?”

Kikuyu: Nao andũ acio magĩcookia atĩrĩ, “Tuumĩte gwa Korinelio, ũrĩa mũnene-wa-thigari-igana rĩmwe. Nĩ mũndũ mũthingu na mwĩtigĩri-Ngai, na nĩatĩĩtwo nĩ Ayahudi othe. Mũraika mũtheru aramwĩrire atũmane ũthiĩ gwake mũciĩ nĩgeetha aigue ũrĩa ũrenda kuuga.”
English: They said, “Cornelius, a centurion, a righteous man and one who fears God, and well spoken of by all the nation of the Jews, was directed by a holy angel to invite you to his house, and to listen to what you say.”

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo Petero akĩĩra andũ acio matoonye nyũmba matuĩke ageni aake. Mũthenya ũyũ ũngĩ warũmĩrĩire Petero agĩthiĩ nao, na ariũ a Ithe witũ amwe kuuma Jopa magĩthiĩ nake.
English: So he called them in and provided a place to stay. On the next day Peter arose and went out with them, and some of the brothers from Joppa accompanied him.

Kikuyu: Naguo mũthenya ũcio ũngĩ warũmĩrĩire magĩkinya Kaisarea. Korinelio agĩkorwo ametereire na eetĩte andũ a nyũmba yake na arata a hakuhĩ.
English: On the next day they entered into Caesarea. Cornelius was waiting for them, having called together his relatives and his near friends.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa Petero aatoonyaga nyũmba, Korinelio akĩmũtũnga na akĩĩgũithia magũrũ-inĩ make amwĩnyiihĩrie.
English: When Peter entered, Cornelius met him, fell down at his feet, and worshiped him.

Kikuyu: No Petero akĩmũũkĩria, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Rũgama, niĩ ndĩ o mũndũ o na niĩ.”
English: But Peter raised him up, saying, “Stand up! I myself am also a man.”

Kikuyu: O makĩaragia-rĩ, Petero agĩtoonya thĩinĩ na agĩkora gĩkundi kĩnene kĩa andũ.
English: As he talked with him, he went in and found many gathered together.

Kikuyu: Akĩmeera atĩrĩ: “Inyuĩ nĩmũũĩ wega atĩ Mũyahudi ndetĩkĩrĩtio nĩ watho kũgwatanĩra na mũndũ wa Ndũrĩrĩ o na kana kũmũceerera. No Ngai nĩanyonetie atĩ ndiagĩrĩirwo nĩ kuuga atĩ mũndũ o na ũrĩkũ ti mwamũre kana atĩ arĩ na thaahu.
English: He said to them, “You yourselves know how it is an unlawful thing for a man who is a Jew to join himself or come to one of another nation, but God has shown me that I shouldn’t call any man unholy or unclean.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio rĩrĩa ndĩratũmanĩirwo, ndĩrokire itegũkararia. Njĩtĩkĩria ngũũrie kĩrĩa kĩratũmire ũndũmanĩre?”
English: Therefore I also came without complaint when I was sent for. I ask therefore, why did you send for me?”

Kikuyu: Nake Korinelio akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ: “Matukũ mana mahĩtũku, ndaarĩ thĩinĩ wa nyũmba yakwa ngĩhooya ithaa-inĩ ta rĩĩrĩ rĩa thaa kenda cia mĩaraho. Mũndũ warĩ na nguo ciahenagia akĩrũgama o rĩmwe mbere yakwa,
English: Cornelius said, “Four days ago, I was fasting until this hour; and at the ninth hour,† I prayed in my house, and behold, a man stood before me in bright clothing

Kikuyu: akĩnjĩĩra atĩrĩ, ‘Korinelio, Ngai nĩaiguĩte mahooya maku, na nĩaririkanĩte iheo ciaku iria wanahe athĩĩni.
English: and said, ‘Cornelius, your prayer is heard, and your gifts to the needy are remembered in the sight of God.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio tũmana Jopa wĩtĩrwo Simoni ũrĩa wĩtagwo Petero, nake nĩ mũgeni gwa Simoni ũrĩa mũtanduki wa njũũa, ũrĩa ũikaraga rũtere-inĩ rwa iria.’
English: Send therefore to Joppa and summon Simon, who is also called Peter. He is staying in the house of a tanner named Simon, by the seaside. When he comes, he will speak to you.’

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio nĩndagũtũmanĩire o hĩndĩ ĩyo, na nĩ weka wega nĩ ũndũ wa gũũka. Rĩu ithuĩ ithuothe tũrĩ haha mbere ya Ngai nĩgeetha tũthikĩrĩrie ũrĩa wothe Mwathani agwathĩte ũtwĩre.”
English: Therefore I sent to you at once, and it was good of you to come. Now therefore we are all here present in the sight of God to hear all things that have been commanded you by God.”

Kikuyu: Nake Petero akĩambĩrĩria kwaria, akiuga atĩrĩ: “Rĩu nĩndamenya kũna ũrĩa arĩ ma atĩ Ngai ndarĩ mũthutũkanio.
English: Peter opened his mouth and said, “Truly I perceive that God doesn’t show favoritism;

Kikuyu: no atĩ nĩetĩkagĩra andũ kuuma ndũrĩrĩ-inĩ ciothe arĩa mamwĩtigagĩra na mageeka ũrĩa kwagĩrĩire.
English: but in every nation he who fears him and works righteousness is acceptable to him.

Kikuyu: Inyuĩ nĩmũũĩ ndũmĩrĩri ĩrĩa Ngai aatũmĩire andũ a Isiraeli, akĩaria ũhoro mwega wa thayũ na ũndũ wa Jesũ Kristũ, ũrĩa arĩ we Mwathani wa andũ othe.
English: The word which he sent to the children of Israel, preaching good news of peace by Jesus Christ—he is Lord of all—

Kikuyu: Inyuĩ nĩmũũĩ ũrĩa gwekĩkire kũu Judea guothe, kwambĩrĩria Galili thuutha wa ũbatithio ũrĩa Johana aahunjagia,
English: you yourselves know what happened, which was proclaimed throughout all Judea, beginning from Galilee, after the baptism which John preached;

Kikuyu: ũrĩa Ngai aaitĩrĩire Jesũ wa Nazarethi Roho Mũtheru o na hinya, na ũrĩa aathiiaga agĩĩkaga wega na akĩhonagia andũ arĩa maahatĩrĩirio nĩ hinya wa mũcukani, tondũ Ngai aarĩ hamwe nake.
English: how God anointed Jesus of Nazareth with the Holy Spirit and with power, who went about doing good and healing all who were oppressed by the devil, for God was with him.

Kikuyu: “Ithuĩ tũrĩ aira a maũndũ marĩa mothe eekire thĩinĩ wa bũrũri wa Ayahudi o na Jerusalemu. Nĩmamũũragire na njĩra ya kũmwamba mũtĩ-igũrũ.
English: We are witnesses of everything he did both in the country of the Jews and in Jerusalem; whom they also‡ killed, hanging him on a tree.

Kikuyu: No mũthenya wa gatatũ Ngai akĩmũriũkia kuuma kũrĩ arĩa akuũ na agĩtũma onekane kũrĩ andũ.
English: God raised him up the third day and gave him to be revealed,

Kikuyu: We ndonirwo nĩ andũ othe, o tiga aira arĩa Ngai aathuurĩte: akĩonwo nĩ ithuĩ, o ithuĩ twarĩĩanĩire na tũkĩnyuuanĩra nake aarĩkia kũriũka kuuma kũrĩ arĩa akuũ.
English: not to all the people, but to witnesses who were chosen before by God, to us,

Kikuyu: Nĩatwathire tũhunjĩrie andũ na tũrute ũira atĩ we nĩ we Ngai aamũrire atuĩke mũtuanĩri-ciira wa andũ arĩa marĩ muoyo na arĩa makuĩte.
English: He commanded us to preach to the people and to testify that this is he who is appointed by God as the Judge of the living and the dead.

Kikuyu: Anabii othe nĩmaheanaga ũira wa ũhoro wake atĩ mũndũ ũrĩa wothe ũkaamwĩtĩkia nĩakamũkĩra ũrekeri wa mehia na ũndũ wa rĩĩtwa rĩake.”
English: All the prophets testify about him, that through his name everyone who believes in him will receive remission of sins.”

Kikuyu: Na Petero o akĩaragia ciugo icio-rĩ, Roho Mũtheru agĩikũrũkĩra andũ arĩa othe maiguire ndũmĩrĩri ĩyo.
English: While Peter was still speaking these words, the Holy Spirit fell on all those who heard the word.

Kikuyu: Nao andũ arĩa meetĩkĩtie, a thiritũ ya arĩa maruaga, arĩa mokĩte na Petero makĩgega mona atĩ kĩheo kĩa Roho Mũtheru nĩgĩaitĩrĩirio andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ o nao.
English: They of the circumcision who believed were amazed, as many as came with Peter, because the gift of the Holy Spirit was also poured out on the Gentiles.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo nĩmamaiguire makĩaria na thiomi makĩgoocaga Ngai. Hĩndĩ ĩyo Petero akĩũria atĩrĩ,
English: For they heard them speaking in other languages and magnifying God. Then Peter answered,

Kikuyu: “Harĩ mũndũ ũngĩgiria andũ aya mabatithio na maaĩ? Andũ aya nĩmamũkĩra Roho Mũtheru o ta ithuĩ.”
English: “Can anyone forbid these people from being baptized with water? They have received the Holy Spirit just like us.”

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio agĩathana mabatithio thĩinĩ wa rĩĩtwa rĩa Jesũ Kristũ. Magĩcooka makĩũria Petero aikaranie nao ihinda rĩa mĩthenya mĩnini. 11
English: He commanded them to be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ. Then they asked him to stay some days.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, atũmwo, na ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ arĩa maarĩ Judea guothe makĩigua atĩ andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ o nao nĩmarĩkĩtie kwamũkĩra kiugo kĩa Ngai.
English: Now the apostles and the brothers† who were in Judea heard that the Gentiles had also received the word of God.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio rĩrĩa Petero aambatire agĩthiĩ Jerusalemu, andũ arĩa meetĩkĩtie a thiritũ ya arĩa maruaga makĩmũrũithia
English: When Peter had come up to Jerusalem, those who were of the circumcision contended with him,

Kikuyu: makĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Wee nĩwatoonyire nyũmba ya andũ arĩa mataruaga, na ũkĩrĩĩanĩra nao.”
English: saying, “You went in to uncircumcised men and ate with them!”

Kikuyu: Nowe Petero akĩambĩrĩria kũmataarĩria maũndũ mothe wega o ta ũrĩa meekĩkĩte, akĩmeera atĩrĩ:
English: But Peter began, and explained to them in order, saying,

Kikuyu: “Ndaarĩ itũũra-inĩ rĩa Jopa ngĩhooya Ngai, na rĩrĩa ndaagoocereire-rĩ, ngĩona kĩoneki. Ngĩona kĩndũ gĩatariĩ ta gĩtama kĩnene gĩkĩharũrũkio kuuma igũrũ na mĩthia yakĩo ĩna, na gĩgĩikũrũka o harĩa ndaarĩ.
English: “I was in the city of Joppa praying, and in a trance I saw a vision: a certain container descending, like it was a great sheet let down from heaven by four corners. It came as far as me.

Kikuyu: Ndaacũthĩrĩria thĩinĩ wakĩo ngĩona nyamũ cia thĩ iria irĩ magũrũ mana, na nyamũ cia gĩthaka, na nyamũ iria itambaga thĩ na nyoni cia rĩera-inĩ.
English: When I had looked intently at it, I considered, and saw the four-footed animals of the earth, wild animals, creeping things, and birds of the sky.

Kikuyu: Ngĩcooka ngĩigua mũgambo ũkĩnjĩĩra atĩrĩ, ‘Petero, ũkĩra. Thĩnja, na ũrĩe!’
English: I also heard a voice saying to me, ‘Rise, Peter, kill and eat!’

Kikuyu: “Na niĩ ngĩcookia atĩrĩ, ‘Aca, Mwathani! Gũtirĩ kĩndũ gĩtarĩ kĩamũre kana kĩrĩ thaahu kĩrĩ gĩatoonya kanua gakwa.’
English: But I said, ‘Not so, Lord, for nothing unholy or unclean has ever entered into my mouth.’

Kikuyu: “Naguo mũgambo ũcio ũkĩaria riita rĩa keerĩ kuuma igũrũ ũkĩnjĩĩra atĩrĩ, ‘Ndũkanoige kĩndũ o na kĩrĩkũ kĩrĩa Ngai atheretie atĩ ti kĩamũre.’
English: But a voice answered me the second time out of heaven, ‘What God has cleansed, don’t you call unclean.’

Kikuyu: Ũndũ ũyũ wekĩkire maita matatũ, nakĩo gĩtama kĩu gĩkĩguucio na igũrũ.
English: This was done three times, and all were drawn up again into heaven.

Kikuyu: “O hĩndĩ ĩyo andũ atatũ arĩa maatũmĩtwo kũrĩ niĩ kuuma Kaisarea magĩkinya nyũmba-inĩ ĩrĩa ndaikaraga.
English: Behold, immediately three men stood before the house where I was, having been sent from Caesarea to me.

Kikuyu: Nake Roho akĩnjĩĩra thiĩ nao na ndikagĩe na ngaanja. Nao ariũ a Ithe witũ aya atandatũ tũgĩthiĩ nao na tũgĩtoonya nyũmba ya mũndũ ũcio.
English: The Spirit told me to go with them without discriminating. These six brothers also accompanied me, and we entered into the man’s house.

Kikuyu: Nake agĩtwĩra ũrĩa oimĩrĩirwo nĩ mũraika arĩ gwake nyũmba, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, ‘Tũmana Jopa wĩtĩrwo Simoni ũrĩa wĩtagwo Petero.
English: He told us how he had seen the angel standing in his house and saying to him, ‘Send to Joppa and get Simon, who is called Peter,

Kikuyu: Nĩegũkũrehera ndũmĩrĩri ĩrĩa ĩgũtũma wee na nyũmba yaku yothe mũhonoke.’
English: who will speak to you words by which you will be saved, you and all your house.’

Kikuyu: “Na rĩrĩa ndaambĩrĩirie kwaria-rĩ, Roho Mũtheru akĩmaikũrũkĩra o ta ũrĩa aatũikũrũkĩire hĩndĩ ya mbere.
English: As I began to speak, the Holy Spirit fell on them, even as on us at the beginning.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo ngĩririkana ũrĩa Mwathani oigĩte atĩrĩ, ‘Johana aabatithanagia na maaĩ, no inyuĩ mũkaabatithio na Roho Mũtheru.’
English: I remembered the word of the Lord, how he said, ‘John indeed baptized in water, but you will be baptized in the Holy Spirit.’

Kikuyu: Angĩkorwo Ngai nĩamaheire kĩheo o kĩrĩa aatũheire ithuĩ andũ arĩa twetĩkirie Mwathani Jesũ Kristũ-rĩ, niĩ ndaarĩ ũũ nĩguo ndĩciirie atĩ no ngararie Ngai?”
English: If then God gave to them the same gift as us when we believed in the Lord Jesus Christ, who was I, that I could withstand God?”

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa maaiguire ũguo, magĩtiga ngarari na makĩgooca Ngai, makiuga atĩrĩ, “Gwakorwo nĩ ũguo-rĩ, Ngai nĩakinyĩirie andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ o nao ũhoro wa kwĩrira nĩguo magĩe na muoyo.”
English: When they heard these things, they held their peace and glorified God, saying, “Then God has also granted to the Gentiles repentance to life!”

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, andũ arĩa maahurunjũkire nĩ ũndũ wa kũnyariirwo thuutha wa kũũragwo gwa Stefano magĩthiĩ o nginya Foinike, na Kuporo, na Antiokia, no matiahunjagĩria andũ angĩ ũhoro tiga o Ayahudi oiki.
English: They therefore who were scattered abroad by the oppression that arose about Stephen traveled as far as Phoenicia, Cyprus, and Antioch, speaking the word to no one except to Jews only.

Kikuyu: O na kũrĩ o ũguo, andũ amwe ao, arĩa moimĩte Kuporo, na Kurene, magĩthiĩ Antiokia na makĩambĩrĩria kwarĩria Ayunani o nao, makĩmahunjĩria ũhoro mwega ũkoniĩ Mwathani Jesũ.
English: But there were some of them, men of Cyprus and Cyrene, who, when they had come to Antioch, spoke to the Hellenists,‡ preaching the Lord Jesus.

Kikuyu: Guoko kwa Mwathani kwarĩ hamwe nao; na andũ aingĩ magĩĩtĩkia makĩgarũrũkĩra Mwathani.
English: The hand of the Lord was with them, and a great number believed and turned to the Lord.

Kikuyu: Nayo ngumo ya maũndũ macio igĩkinyĩra andũ a kanitha ũrĩa warĩ kũu Jerusalemu; nao magĩtũma Baranaba athiĩ Antiokia.
English: The report concerning them came to the ears of the assembly which was in

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa aakinyire na akĩona ũira wa wega wa Ngai, agĩkena na akĩmomĩrĩria marĩ othe, atĩ maikarage maathĩkĩire Mwathani na ngoro ciao ciothe.
English: who, when he had come, and had seen the grace of God, was glad. He exhorted them all, that with purpose of heart they should remain near to the Lord.

Kikuyu: Baranaba aarĩ mũndũ mwega waiyũrĩtwo nĩ Roho Mũtheru na warĩ na wĩtĩkio, nao andũ aingĩ magĩĩtĩkia Mwathani.
English: For he was a good man, and full of the Holy Spirit and of faith, and many people were added to the Lord.

Kikuyu: Nake Baranaba agĩthiĩ Tariso gũcaria Saũlũ,
English: Barnabas went out to Tarsus to look for Saul.

Kikuyu: na rĩrĩa aamuonire akĩmũrehe Antiokia. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio kwa ihinda rĩa mwaka ũmwe Baranaba na Saũlũ makĩgomana na kanitha na makĩruta andũ aingĩ. Kũu Antiokia nĩkuo arutwo meetirwo Akristiano riita rĩa mbere.
English: When he had found him, he brought him to Antioch. For a whole year they were gathered together with the assembly, and taught many people. The disciples were first called Christians in Antioch.

Kikuyu: Ihinda-inĩ rĩu anabii amwe magĩikũrũka kuuma Jerusalemu nginya Antiokia.
English: Now in these days, prophets came down from Jerusalem to Antioch.

Kikuyu: Ũmwe wao wetagwo Agabo akĩrũgama, na nĩ ũndũ wa kũmenyithio nĩ Roho Mũtheru, akĩratha atĩ nĩ gũkũgĩa ngʼaragu nene Roma guothe. (Ũndũ ũcio wekĩkire hĩndĩ ya wathani wa Kilaudio.)
English: One of them named Agabus stood up and indicated by the Spirit that there should be a great famine all over the world, which also happened in the days of Claudius.

Kikuyu: Nao arutwo, magĩĩciiria kũheana ũteithio nĩ ũndũ wa ariũ a Ithe witũ arĩa maatũũraga kũu Judea, o ta ũrĩa mũndũ aahotithĩtio.
English: As any of the disciples had plenty, each determined to send relief to the brothers who lived in Judea;

Kikuyu: Nĩmekire ũndũ ũcio, makĩnengera Baranaba na Saũlũ iheo ciao matwarĩre athuuri a kanitha.
English: which they also did, sending it to the elders by the hands of Barnabas and Saul.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, ihinda-inĩ rĩu nĩguo Mũthamaki Herode aanyiitire andũ amwe a kanitha, arĩ na muoroto wa kũmanyariira.
English: Now about that time, King Herod stretched out his hands to oppress some of the assembly.

Kikuyu: Akĩũragithia Jakubu, mũrũ wa nyina na Johana na rũhiũ rwa njora.
English: He killed James, the brother of John, with the sword.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa
English: When he saw that it pleased the Jews, he proceeded to seize Peter also. This was during the days of unleavened bread.

Kikuyu: Aarĩkia kũmũnyiitithia, akĩmũikia njeera akĩmũneana arangĩrwo nĩ ikundi inya cia thigari, o gĩkundi kĩrĩ gĩa thigari inya. Herode eendaga amũrehe mbere ya mũingĩ thuutha wa Gĩathĩ gĩa Bathaka.
English: When he had arrested him, he put him in prison and delivered him to four squads of four soldiers each to guard him, intending to bring him out to the people after the Passover.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Petero akĩhingĩrwo njeera, no kanitha nĩwahooyaga Ngai ũtegũtigithĩria nĩ ũndũ wake.
English: Peter therefore was kept in the prison, but constant prayer was made by the assembly to God for him.

Kikuyu: Na ũtukũ ũrĩa Herode aarokaga kũmũtwara agaciirithio, Petero aakomete gatagatĩ ga thigari igĩrĩ, ohetwo na mĩnyororo ĩĩrĩ, nacio thigari igĩrĩ ikarũgama kĩhingo-inĩ irangĩrĩte.
English: The same night when Herod was about to bring him out, Peter was sleeping between two soldiers, bound with two chains. Guards in front of the door kept the prison.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, o rĩmwe mũraika wa Mwathani akĩmuumĩrĩra naguo ũtheri ũkĩara kanyũmba kau aarĩ. Mũraika ũcio akĩringaringa Petero mwena na akĩmũũkĩria, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Ũkĩra narua!” Nayo mĩnyororo ĩrĩa oohetwo nayo moko ĩkĩgũa thĩ.
English: And behold, an angel of the Lord stood by him, and a light shone in the cell. He struck Peter on the side and woke him up, saying, “Stand up quickly!” His chains fell off his hands.

Kikuyu: Mũraikaũcioagĩcookaakĩmwĩraatĩrĩ, “Wĩhumbenguociakunawĩkĩreiraatũ.” NakePeteroagĩĩka o ũguo. Mũraika ũcio akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Wĩhotore nguo yaku na ũnũmĩrĩre.”
English: The angel said to him, “Get dressed and put on your sandals.” He did so. He said to him, “Put on your cloak and follow me.”

Kikuyu: Petero akĩmũrũmĩrĩra, makiuma nja ya njeera, no ndaamenyaga atĩ ũndũ ũcio mũraika eekaga warĩ ũndũ wa ma; eeciiragia atĩ nĩ kĩoneki oonaga.
English: And he went out and followed him. He didn’t know that what was being done by the angel was real, but thought he saw a vision.

Kikuyu: Makĩhĩtũka arangĩri a mbere na a keerĩ na magĩkinya kĩhingo-inĩ gĩa kĩgera kĩrĩa gĩa gũtoonya itũũra inene. Nakĩo kĩhingo kĩu gĩkĩĩhingũra nĩ ũndũ wao, nao makĩgerera ho. Rĩrĩa maathiire ũraihu wa gacĩra kamwe-rĩ, o rĩmwe mũraika ũcio akĩmũtiga.
English: When they were past the first and the second guard, they came to the iron gate that leads into the city, which opened to them by itself. They went out and went down one street, and immediately the angel departed from him.

Kikuyu: Nake Petero akĩĩigua, na akiuga atĩrĩ, “Rĩu nĩndamenya hatarĩ nganja atĩ Mwathani nĩegũtũmĩte mũraika wake aahonokie kuuma moko-inĩ ma Herode na kuuma kũrĩ maũndũ marĩa mothe andũ a Ayahudi matanyĩte kuona.”
English: When Peter had come to himself, he said, “Now I truly know that the Lord has sent out his angel and delivered me out of the hand of Herod, and from everything the Jewish people were expecting.”

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa aguũkĩirwo nĩ ũhoro ũcio, agĩthiĩ kwa Mariamu ũrĩa warĩ nyina wa Johana, na nowe wetagwo Mariko, na nĩkuo andũ aingĩ monganĩte makĩhooya Ngai.
English: Thinking about that, he came to the house of Mary, the mother of John who was called Mark, where many were gathered together and were praying.

Kikuyu: Petero akĩringaringa mũrango wa nja, nake mũirĩtu warutaga wĩra kuo wetagwo Roda agĩũka kũhingũra.
English: When Peter knocked at the door of the gate, a servant girl named Rhoda came to answer.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa aaiguire mũgambo wa Petero, akĩiyũrwo nĩ gĩkeno kĩnene akĩaga kũhingũra mũrango akĩhũndũka atengʼerete, akĩanĩrĩra, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Petero arĩ mũrango-inĩ!”
English: When she recognized Peter’s voice, she didn’t open the gate for joy, but ran in and reported that Peter was standing in front of the gate.

Kikuyu: Nao makĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Wee nĩ kũgũrũka ũgũrũkĩte.” Rĩrĩa aakĩrĩrĩirie agwatĩirie kuuga atĩ ũguo nĩguo kwarĩ, makiuga atĩrĩ, “No nginya akorwo nĩ mũraika wake.”
English: They said to her, “You are crazy!” But she insisted that it was so. They said, “It is his angel.”

Kikuyu: Nowe Petero agĩthiĩ na mbere kũringaringa mũrango, na rĩrĩa maahingũrire makĩona nĩwe, makĩgega.
English: But Peter continued knocking. When they had opened, they saw him and were amazed.

Kikuyu: Petero akĩmakiria na guoko na akĩmataarĩria ũrĩa Mwathani aamũrutĩte njeera. Akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Ĩrai Jakubu na ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ ũhoro ũcio,” agĩcooka agĩthiĩ kũndũ kũngĩ.
English: But he, beckoning to them with his hand to be silent, declared to them how the Lord had brought him out of the prison. He said, “Tell these things to James and to the brothers.” Then he departed and went to another place.

Kikuyu: Rũciinĩ gwakĩa, nĩ kwagĩire na ngũĩ nene gatagatĩ-inĩ ga thigari nĩ kũrigwo nĩ ũrĩa gwekĩkĩte ũhoro ũkoniĩ Petero.
English: Now as soon as it was day, there was no small stir among the soldiers about what had become of Peter.

Kikuyu: Thuutha wa Herode gũcaria Petero na kũmwaga-rĩ, agĩciirithia thigari, na agĩathana atĩ ciũragwo. Herode agĩcooka akiuma Judea agĩthiĩ Kaisarea, agĩikara kuo kwa ihinda inini.
English: When Herod had sought for him and didn’t find him, he examined the guards, then commanded that they should be put to death. He went down from Judea to Caesarea, and stayed there.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, nĩatũũrĩte ahaaranĩte na andũ a Turo na a Sidoni, no o magĩcookanĩrĩra hamwe magĩetha ũrĩa mangĩaranĩirie nake. Na maarĩkia kũnyiitwo mbaru nĩ Bulasito, ũrĩa warĩ ndungata njĩhokeku ya mũthamaki-rĩ, makĩhooya kũgĩe na thayũ, tondũ bũrũri wa mũthamaki ũcio nĩguo wamaheaga irio.
English: Now Herod was very angry with the people of Tyre and Sidon. They came with one accord to him and, having made Blastus, the king’s personal aide, their friend, they asked for peace, because their country depended on the king’s country for food.

Kikuyu: Na mũthenya ũrĩa waraarĩirio wakinya-rĩ, Herode akĩĩhumba nguo ciake cia ũthamaki, na agĩikarĩra gĩtĩ gĩake kĩa ũnene, akĩarĩria andũ acio.
English: On an appointed day, Herod dressed himself in royal clothing, sat on the throne, and gave a speech to them.

Kikuyu: Nao makĩanĩrĩra makiuga atĩrĩ, “Ũcio nĩ mũgambo wa ngai, ti mũgambo wa mũndũ.”
English: The people shouted, “The voice of a god, and not of a man!”

Kikuyu: O rĩmwe mũraika wa Mwathani akĩringa Herode nĩ ũndũ wa kwaga kũgoocithia Ngai, nake akĩrĩĩo nĩ igunyũ, agĩkua.
English: Immediately an angel of the Lord struck him, because he didn’t give God the glory. Then he was eaten by worms and died.

Kikuyu: No kiugo kĩa Ngai gĩgĩkĩrĩrĩria kuongerereka na gũtheerema.
English: But the word of God grew and multiplied.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Baranaba na Saũlũ maarĩkirie ũtungata wao-rĩ, magĩcooka kuuma Jerusalemu, na magĩ- cooka na Johana, na nowe wetagwo Mariko.
English: Barnabas and Saul returned to† Jerusalem when they had fulfilled their service, also taking with them John who was called Mark.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, kanitha-inĩ wa Antiokia nĩ kwarĩ na anabii na arutani: nao nĩo Baranaba, na Simeoni ũrĩa wetagwo na rĩĩtwa rĩngĩ Nigeri, na Lukio ũrĩa woimĩte Kurene, na Manaeni (ũrĩa wareranĩirio
English: Now in the assembly that was at Antioch there were some prophets and teachers: Barnabas, Simeon who was called Niger, Lucius of Cyrene, Manaen the foster brother of Herod the tetrarch, and Saul.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa mahooyaga Mwathani mehingĩte kũrĩa irio, Roho Mũtheru akiuga atĩrĩ, “Nyamũrĩrai Baranaba na Saũlũ nĩ ũndũ wa wĩra ũrĩa ndĩmetĩire.”
English: As they served the Lord and fasted, the Holy Spirit said, “Separate Barnabas and Saul for me, for the work to which I have called them.”

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio thuutha wa kwĩhinga kũrĩa irio na kũhooya Ngai, makĩmaigĩrĩra moko, na makĩmatũma mathiĩ.
English: Then, when they had fasted and prayed and laid their hands on them, they sent them away.

Kikuyu: Andũ acio eerĩ, matũmĩtwo nĩ Roho Mũtheru, magĩthiĩ nginya Seleukia na makĩhaica marikabu makiuma kũu magĩthiĩ nginya Kuporo.
English: So, being sent out by the Holy Spirit, they went down to Seleucia. From there they sailed to Cyprus.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa maakinyire Salamisi, makĩhunjia kiugo kĩa Ngai thunagogi-inĩ cia Ayahudi. Nake Johana aarĩ hamwe nao arĩ ta mũteithia wao.
English: When they were at Salamis, they proclaimed God’s word in the Jewish synagogues. They also had John as their attendant.

Kikuyu: Magĩthiĩ matuĩkanĩirie gĩcigĩrĩra kĩu gĩothe nginya magĩkinya Pafo. Kũu magĩkora Mũyahudi warĩ mũragũri na mũnabii wa maheeni wetagwo Bari-Jesũ,
English: When they had gone through the island to Paphos, they found a certain sorcerer, a false prophet, a Jew whose name was Bar Jesus,

Kikuyu: nake aatungatagĩra barũthi ũrĩa wetagwo Serigio Paũlũ. Barũthi ũcio warĩ mũndũ mũũgĩ, agĩtũmanĩra Baranaba na Saũlũ tondũ nĩendaga kũigua kiugo kĩa Ngai.
English: who was with the proconsul, Sergius Paulus, a man of understanding. This man summoned Barnabas and Saul, and sought to hear the word of God.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, Elumo ũcio mũragũri (amu ũguo nĩguo rĩĩtwa rĩake riugĩte) akĩmakararia na akĩgeria kũgarũra barũthi ũcio nĩgeetha ndagetĩkie.
English: But Elymas the sorcerer (for so is his name by interpretation) withstood them, seeking to turn the proconsul away from the faith.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo Saũlũ, ũrĩa wetagwo na rĩĩtwa rĩngĩ Paũlũ aiyũrĩtwo nĩ Roho Mũtheru, agĩkũũrĩra Elumo maitho, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ,
English: But Saul, who is also called Paul, filled with the Holy Spirit, fastened his eyes on him

Kikuyu: “Wee ũrĩ mwana wa mũcukani na ũrĩ thũ ya maũndũ marĩa mothe mega! Ũiyũrĩtwo nĩ mĩthemba yothe ya maheeni na waara. Ndũrĩ hĩndĩ ũgaatiga kuogomia njĩra iria ciagĩrĩire cia Mwathani?
English: and said, “You son of the devil, full of all deceit and all cunning, you enemy of all righteousness, will you not cease to pervert the right ways of the Lord?

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, guoko kwa Mwathani nĩgũgũũkĩrĩire. Nĩũgũtuĩka mũtumumu na kwa ihinda ndũgũkorwo ũkĩona ũtheri wa riũa.” O hĩndĩ ĩyo akĩona marundurundu, na akĩgĩĩo nĩ nduma, akĩambĩrĩria kũhambahambata, agĩcaragia mũndũ wa kũmũnyiita guoko amũtongorie.
English: Now, behold, the hand of the Lord is on you, and you will be blind, not seeing the sun for a season!” Immediately a mist and darkness fell on him. He went around seeking someone to lead him by the hand.

Kikuyu: Nake barũthi oona ũrĩa gwekĩka, agĩĩtĩkia, nĩgũkorwo nĩagegirio nĩ ũrutani ũcio ũkoniĩ Mwathani.
English: Then the proconsul, when he saw what was done, believed, being astonished at the teaching of the Lord.

Kikuyu: Paũlũ na andũ arĩa maarĩ nao, moima Pafo makĩhaica marikabu magĩthiĩ Periga kũu bũrũri wa Pamufilia, kũrĩa Johana aamatigire agĩcooka Jerusalemu.
English: Now Paul and his company set sail from Paphos and came to Perga in Pamphylia. John departed from them and returned to Jerusalem.

Kikuyu: Moima Periga, magĩthiĩ na mbere magĩkinya Antiokia ya bũrũri wa Pisidia. Na mũthenya wa Thabatũ magĩtoonya thunagogi magĩikara thĩ.
English: But they, passing on from Perga, came to Antioch of Pisidia. They went into the synagogue on the Sabbath day and sat down.

Kikuyu: Thuutha wa gũthomwo kwa Maandĩko ma Watho na ma Anabii, anene a thunagogi makĩmatũmanĩra makĩmeera atĩrĩ; “Ariũ a Ithe witũ, angĩkorwo mũrĩ na ũhoro wa kũũmĩrĩria andũ- rĩ, maarĩriei.”
English: After the reading of the law and the prophets, the rulers of the synagogue sent to them, saying, “Brothers, if you have any word of exhortation for the people, speak.”

Kikuyu: Paũlũ akĩrũgama, na aarĩkia kũmakiria na guoko akĩmeera atĩrĩ: “Andũ aya a Isiraeli o na inyuĩ andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ arĩa mũhooyaga Ngai, ta thikĩrĩriai!
English: Paul stood up, and gesturing with his hand said, “Men of Israel, and you who fear God, listen.

Kikuyu: Ngai wa andũ a Isiraeli nĩathuurire maithe maitũ; agĩtũma andũ acio matheereme hĩndĩ ĩrĩa maatũũraga Misiri, na akĩmaruta bũrũri ũcio na hinya mũnene,
English: The God of this people† chose our fathers, and exalted the people when they stayed as aliens in the land of Egypt, and with an uplifted arm, he led them out of it.

Kikuyu: nĩakirĩrĩirie mĩtugo yao ihinda rĩa ta mĩaka mĩrongo ĩna marĩ werũ-inĩ,
English: For a period of about forty years he put up with them in the wilderness.

Kikuyu: akĩniina ndũrĩrĩ mũgwanja kũu Kaanani naguo bũrũri wacio akĩũhe andũ aake ũtuĩke igai rĩao.
English: When he had destroyed seven nations in the land of Canaan, he gave

Kikuyu: Ũhoro ũcio wothe wekĩkire ihinda-inĩ rĩa ta mĩaka magana mana ma mĩrongo ĩtano. “Thuutha wa ũguo, Ngai akĩmahe atiirĩrĩri bũrũri o nginya hĩndĩ ya Samũeli ũrĩa warĩ mũnabii.
English: After these things, he gave them judges until Samuel the prophet.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo andũ makiuga maheo mũthamaki, nake Ngai akĩmahe Saũlũ mũrũ wa Kishu, wa mũhĩrĩga wa Benjamini, nake agĩathana mĩaka mĩrongo ĩna.
English: Afterward they asked for a king, and God gave to them Saul the son of Kish, a man of the tribe of Benjamin, for forty years.

Kikuyu: Thuutha wa kweheria Saũlũ, agĩtua Daudi mũthamaki wao. Akĩruta ũira ũmũkoniĩ, akiuga atĩrĩ, ‘Nĩnyonete Daudi mũrũ wa Jesii arĩ mũndũ ũkenagia ngoro yakwa; we nĩarĩĩkaga ũrĩa wothe ngwenda eke.’
English: When he had removed him, he raised up David to be their king, to whom he also testified, ‘I have found David the son of Jesse, a man after my heart, who will do all my will.’

Kikuyu: “Kuuma rũciaro-inĩ rwa mũndũ ũyũ, Ngai nĩareheire andũ a Isiraeli Mũhonokia, na nĩwe Jesũ, o ta ũrĩa eeranĩire.
English: From this man’s offspring, God has brought salvation‡ to Israel according to his promise,

Kikuyu: Jesũ atanooka, Johana nĩahunjagĩria andũ othe a Isiraeli ũhoro wa kwĩrira mehia na ũhoro wa kũbatithio.
English: before his coming, when John had first preached the baptism of repentance to Israel.§

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa Johana aarĩkagia wĩra wake nĩoririe atĩrĩ: ‘Mwĩciiragia niĩ nĩ niĩ ũũ? Niĩ ti niĩ we. Aca niĩ ti niĩ, nowe nĩegũũka thuutha wakwa, o we ũrĩa niĩ itagĩrĩirwo kuohora ndigi cia iraatũ ciake.’
English: As John was fulfilling his course, he said, ‘What do you suppose that I am? I am not he. But behold, one comes after me, the sandals of whose feet I am not worthy to untie.’

Kikuyu: “Ariũ a Ithe witũ, o inyuĩ ciana cia Iburahĩmu, o na inyuĩ andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ arĩa mwĩtigĩrĩte Ngai, ũhoro ũyũ wa ũhonokio nĩ ithuĩ tũtũmĩirwo.
English: “Brothers, children of the stock of Abraham, and those among you who fear God, the word of this salvation is sent out to you.

Kikuyu: Andũ a Jerusalemu na anene ao matiakũũranire Jesũ, no nĩ ũndũ wa ũrĩa maamũtuĩrĩire ciira, makĩhingia ciugo cia anabii iria ithomagwo o mũthenya wa Thabatũ.
English: For those who dwell in Jerusalem, and their rulers, because they didn’t know him, nor the voices of the prophets which are read every Sabbath, fulfilled them by condemning him.

Kikuyu: O na gũtuĩka mationire gĩtũmi kĩiganu gĩa gũtũma ooragwo, nĩmathaithire Pilato aathane ooragwo.
English: Though they found no cause for death, they still asked Pilate to have him killed.

Kikuyu: Na maarĩkia kũhingia maũndũ mothe marĩa maandĩkĩtwo ũhoro wake, makĩmũcurũria kuuma mũtĩ-igũrũ, makĩmũiga mbĩrĩra.
English: When they had fulfilled all things that were written about him, they took him down from the tree and laid him in a tomb.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, Ngai nĩamũriũkirie kuuma kũrĩ arĩa akuũ,
English: But God raised him from the dead,

Kikuyu: na
English: and he was seen for many days by those who came up with him from Galilee to Jerusalem, who are his witnesses to the people.

Kikuyu: “Tũramwĩra ũhoro mwega: Ũhoro ũrĩa Ngai eerĩire maithe maitũ,
English: We bring you good news of the promise made to the fathers,

Kikuyu: nĩatũhingĩirie ithuĩ ciana ciao, na ũndũ wa kũriũkia Jesũ. O ta ũrĩa kwandĩkĩtwo thĩinĩ wa Thaburi ya keerĩ atĩrĩ: “ ‘Wee ũrĩ Mũrũ wakwa; ũmũthĩ nĩndatuĩka Thoguo.’
English: that God has fulfilled this to us, their children, in that he raised up Jesus. As it is also written in the second psalm, ‘You are my Son. Today I have become your father.’*

Kikuyu: Naguo ũhoro wa ma wa atĩ Ngai nĩamũriũkirie kuuma kũrĩ arĩa akuũ nĩguo ndakabuthe-rĩ, nĩ warĩtio na ciugo ici: “ ‘Nĩngakũhe irathimo iria theru na iria itarĩ hĩndĩ ingĩaga iria cierĩirwo Daudi.’
English: “Concerning that he raised him up from the dead, now no more to return to corruption, he has spoken thus: ‘I will give you the holy and sure blessings of David.’*

Kikuyu: Ũndũ ũcio nĩwarĩtio handũ hangĩ atĩrĩ: “ ‘Wee ndũkareka ũrĩa waku Mũtheru abuthe.’
English: Therefore he says also in another psalm, ‘You will not allow your Holy One to see decay.’*

Kikuyu: “Nĩgũkorwo rĩrĩa Daudi aarĩkirie gũtungatĩra Ngai rũciaro-inĩ rwake, nĩakuire; agĩthikwo hamwe na maithe make naguo mwĩrĩ wake ũkĩbutha.
English: For David, after he had in his own generation served the counsel of God, fell asleep, was laid with his fathers, and saw decay.

Kikuyu: No ũrĩa Ngai aariũkirie kuuma kũrĩ arĩa akuũ ndaabuthire.
English: But he whom God raised up saw no decay.

Kikuyu: “Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, ariũ a Ithe witũ, ngwenda mũmenye atĩ ũhoro wa kũrekerwo kwa mehia nĩũhunjĩtio kũrĩ inyuĩ na ũndũ wa Jesũ.
English: Be it known to you therefore, brothers,† that through this man is proclaimed to you remission of sins;

Kikuyu: Na mũndũ wothe ũmwĩtĩkĩtie nĩatuuagwo mũthingu maũndũ-inĩ mothe marĩa watho wa Musa ũtangĩamũtuire mũthingu.
English: and by him everyone who believes is justified from all things, from which you could not be justified by the law of Moses.

Kikuyu: Mwĩmenyererei, nĩgeetha ũhoro ũrĩa anabii maaririe ndũkanamũkore, rĩrĩa moigire atĩrĩ:
English: Beware therefore, lest that come on you which is spoken in the prophets:

Kikuyu: “ ‘Ta rorai, inyuĩ anyũrũrania, gegaai na mũthire, nĩgũkorwo nĩngwĩka ũndũ matukũ-inĩ manyu, ũrĩa mũtangĩtĩkia, o na mũngĩĩrwo nĩ mũndũ.’ ”
English: ‘Behold, you scoffers! Wonder and perish, for I work a work in your days, a work which you will in no way believe, if one declares it to you.’ ”*

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Paũlũ na Baranaba moimaga thunagogi, andũ makĩmeera moke mũthenya ũrĩa warũmĩrĩire wa Thabatũ nĩguo mathiĩ na mbere kwaria ũhoro ũcio.
English: So when the Jews went out of the synagogue, the Gentiles begged that these words might be preached to them the next Sabbath.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa mũcemanio wathirire, Ayahudi aingĩ na andũ arĩa meegarũrĩte magatuĩka Ayahudi makĩrũmĩrĩra Paũlũ na Baranaba, nao makĩmaarĩria na makĩmaringĩrĩria mathiĩ na mbere gũikara wega-inĩ wa Ngai.
English: Now when the synagogue broke up, many of the Jews and of the devout proselytes followed Paul and Barnabas; who, speaking to them, urged them to continue in the grace of God.

Kikuyu: Mũthenya ũcio ũngĩ warũmĩrĩire wa Thabatũ, hakuhĩ andũ othe a itũũra rĩu inene magĩũka kũigua kiugo kĩa Mwathani.
English: The next Sabbath, almost the whole city was gathered together to hear the word of God.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa Ayahudi moonire kĩrĩndĩ kĩu, makĩnyiitwo nĩ ũiru na makĩaria ciugo njũru cia gũũkĩrĩra ũrĩa Paũlũ oigaga.
English: But when the Jews saw the multitudes, they were filled with jealousy, and contradicted the things which were spoken by Paul, and blasphemed.

Kikuyu: Nao Paũlũ na Baranaba makĩmacookeria marĩ na ũcamba makĩmeera atĩrĩ, “No nginya tũngĩram- bire kũheana kiugo kĩa Ngai kũrĩ inyuĩ. No rĩrĩ, kuona nĩmũraũrega na mũgetuĩra atĩ mũtiagĩrĩirwo nĩ muoyo wa tene na tene-rĩ, rĩu nĩtũkũgarũrũka tũthiĩ kũrĩ andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ.
English: Paul and Barnabas spoke out boldly, and said, “It was necessary that God’s word should be spoken to you first. Since indeed you thrust it from yourselves, and judge yourselves unworthy of eternal life, behold, we turn to the Gentiles.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Mwathani atwathĩte agatwĩra atĩrĩ: “ ‘Nĩndĩmũtuĩte ũtheri wa gũtherera andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ, nĩguo mũtware ũhoro wa ũhonokio nginya ituri-inĩ cia thĩ.’ ”
English: For so has the Lord commanded us, saying, ‘I have set you as a light for the Gentiles, that you should bring salvation to the uttermost parts of the earth.’ ”*

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ maaiguire ũguo magĩkena na magĩtĩĩa kiugo kĩa Mwathani, nao andũ arĩa othe maathĩrĩirio muoyo-inĩ wa tene na tene magĩĩtĩkia.
English: As the Gentiles heard this, they were glad and glorified the word of God. As many as were appointed to eternal life believed.

Kikuyu: Nakĩo kiugo kĩa Mwathani gĩkĩhunja kũndũ guothe kũu bũrũri-inĩ.
English: The Lord’s word was spread abroad throughout all the region.

Kikuyu: No Ayahudi magĩakĩrĩria atumia arĩa meyamũrĩire Ngai na maarĩ igweta, o na anene a itũũra inene. Makĩrutithia ngũĩ ya gũũkĩrĩra Paũlũ na Baranaba nĩguo manyariirwo, na makĩmaingata moime bũrũri-inĩ wao.
English: But the Jews stirred up the devout and prominent women and the chief men of the city, and stirred up a persecution against Paul and Barnabas, and threw them out of their borders.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio makĩribariba rũkũngũ kuuma magũrũ mao kuonania, atĩ nĩmatigana nao na magĩthiĩ Ikonia.
English: But they shook off the dust of their feet against them, and came to Iconium.

Kikuyu: Nao arutwo makĩiyũrwo ni gĩkeno, na makĩiyũrwo nĩ Roho Mũtheru.
English: The disciples were filled with joy and with the Holy Spirit.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, Paũlũ na Baranaba marĩ kũu Ikonia, magĩtoonya thunagogi ya Ayahudi o ta mũtugo. Marĩ kũu makĩaria na ũhoti mũingĩ mũno o nginya Ayahudi aingĩ na andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ magĩĩtĩkia.
English: In Iconium, they entered together into the synagogue of the Jews, and so spoke that a great multitude both of Jews and of Greeks believed.

Kikuyu: No Ayahudi arĩa maaregire gwĩtĩkia nĩmathogothire andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ, makĩmathũkia meciiria nĩgeetha mookĩrĩre ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ.
English: But the disbelieving† Jews stirred up and embittered the souls of the Gentiles against the brothers.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Paũlũ na Baranaba magĩikara kũu ihinda iraaya, makĩaria marĩ na ũcamba nĩ ũndũ wa Mwathani, o we wekĩrire ndũmĩrĩri ya wega wake hinya na
English: Therefore they stayed there a long time, speaking boldly in the Lord, who testified to the word of his grace, granting signs and wonders to be done by their hands.

Kikuyu: Andũ a itũũra rĩu inene makĩgayũkana; amwe magĩĩtĩkania na Ayahudi, nao arĩa angĩ magĩĩtĩkania na atũmwo.
English: But the multitude of the city was divided. Part sided with the Jews and part with the apostles.

Kikuyu: Nĩ kwarĩ na ndundu yathugundĩtwo nĩ andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ na Ayahudi, hamwe na atongoria ao, nĩguo mameeke ũũru, na mamahũũre na mahiga nyuguto.
English: When some of both the Gentiles and the Jews, with their rulers, made a violent attempt to mistreat and stone them,

Kikuyu: No nao makĩmenya ũhoro ũcio, na makĩũrĩra matũũra manene ma bũrũri wa Likaonia, na nĩmo Lisitera, na Deribe, o na bũrũri ũrĩa wagũthiũrũrũkĩirie,
English: they became aware of it and fled to the cities of Lycaonia, Lystra, Derbe, and the surrounding region.

Kikuyu: kũrĩa maathiire na mbere kũhunjia ũhoro mwega.
English: There they preached the Good News.

Kikuyu: Nakuo kũu Lisitera nĩ kwarĩ mũndũ warĩ mwonju magũrũ, na aatũũrĩte arĩ mwonju kuuma gũciarwo na ndetwarĩte na magũrũ o narĩ.
English: At Lystra a certain man sat, impotent in his feet, a cripple from his mother’s womb, who never had walked.

Kikuyu: Nake agĩthikĩrĩria Paũlũ akĩaria. Paũlũ aamũrora, akĩona atĩ aarĩ na wĩtĩkio wa kũhonio,
English: He was listening to Paul speaking, who, fastening eyes on him and seeing that he had faith to be made whole,

Kikuyu: akĩmwĩta na mũgambo mũnene, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Rũgama na magũrũ maku!” Na o hĩndĩ ĩyo, mũndũ ũcio akĩrũga na igũrũ, akĩambĩrĩria gwĩtwara.
English: said with a loud voice, “Stand upright on your feet!” He leaped up and walked.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa gĩkundi kĩu kĩa andũ kĩonire ũrĩa Paũlũ eekĩte, gĩkĩanĩrĩra na rũthiomi rwa Likaonia gĩkiuga atĩrĩ, “Ngai nĩciikũrũkĩte kũrĩ ithuĩ itariĩ ta andũ!”
English: When the multitude saw what Paul had done, they lifted up their voice, saying in the language of Lycaonia, “The gods have come down to us in the likeness of men!”

Kikuyu: Magĩĩta Baranaba Zeu,* nake Paũlũ makĩmwĩta Herime tondũ nĩwe warĩ mwaria ũrĩa mũnene.
English: They called Barnabas “Jupiter”, and Paul “Mercury”, because he was the chief speaker.

Kikuyu: Mũthĩnjĩri-ngai ya Zeu, ũrĩa hekarũ yake yarĩ o hau nja ya itũũra, akĩrehe ndegwa na mahũa maatumĩtwo ta thũmbĩ hau kĩhingo-inĩ gĩa itũũra inene, tondũ we na gĩkundi kĩu kĩa andũ nĩmendaga kũmarutĩra magongona.
English: The priest of Jupiter, whose temple was in front of their city, brought oxen and garlands to the gates, and would have made a sacrifice along with the multitudes.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩa atũmwo acio Baranaba na Paũlũ maaiguire ũguo, magĩtembũranga nguo ciao, makĩhanyũka, magĩtoonya kũrĩ kĩrĩndĩ, makĩanagĩrĩra atĩrĩ,
English: But when the apostles, Barnabas and Paul, heard of it, they tore their clothes and sprang into the multitude, crying out,

Kikuyu: “Andũ aya, mũreka ũguo nĩkĩ? Ithuĩ tũrĩ o andũ ta inyuĩ. Tũmũreheire ũhoro mwega, wa kũmwĩra mũgarũrũke mũtigane na maũndũ marĩa matarĩ kĩene, mũcookerere Ngai ũrĩa ũtũũraga muoyo, o we ũrĩa wombire igũrũ na thĩ na iria na indo ciothe iria irĩ kuo.
English: “Men, why are you doing these things? We also are men of the same nature as you, and bring you good news, that you should turn from these vain things to the living God, who made the sky, the earth, the sea, and all that is in them;

Kikuyu: Tene, nĩarekereirie ndũrĩrĩ ciothe ithiiage na mĩthiĩre yacio nyene,
English: who in the generations gone by allowed all the nations to walk in their own ways.

Kikuyu: no ndaigana gwĩtiga atarĩ na ũira wa kuonania atĩ arĩ kuo: nĩamuonetie ũtugi na ũndũ wa kũmuurĩria mbura yumĩte igũrũ na kũmũhe magetha hĩndĩ ya kĩmera; nĩamũheaga irio nyingĩ na akaiyũria ngoro cianyu na gĩkeno.”
English: Yet he didn’t leave himself without witness, in that he did good and gave you‡ rains from the sky and fruitful seasons, filling our hearts with food and gladness.”

Kikuyu: No o na maarĩtie ciugo icio, nĩ maarĩ na wĩra mũritũ wa kũgiria gĩkundi kĩu kĩa andũ kĩmarutĩre igongona.
English: Even saying these things, they hardly stopped the multitudes from making a sacrifice to them.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, hĩndĩ ĩyo Ayahudi angĩ magĩũka moimĩte Antiokia na Ikonia, nao makĩguucĩrĩria gĩkundi kĩu kĩa andũ, gĩgĩcooka mwena wao. Gĩkundi kĩu gĩkĩhũũra Paũlũ na mahiga nyuguto, na gĩkĩmũkururia, gĩkĩmũruta nja ya itũũra, gĩĩciirĩtie nĩ mũkuũ.
English: But some Jews from Antioch and Iconium came there, and having persuaded the multitudes, they stoned Paul and dragged him out of the city, supposing that he was dead.

Kikuyu: No arutwo maarĩkia kũmũrigiicĩria, agĩũkĩra agĩcooka itũũra-inĩ. Mũthenya ũcio ũngĩ warũmĩrĩire, we na Baranaba makiuma kũu magĩthiĩ Deribe.
English: But as the disciples stood around him, he rose up, and entered into the city. On the next day he went out with Barnabas to Derbe.

Kikuyu: Paũlũ wa Baranaba makĩhunjia ũhoro mwega itũũra-inĩ rĩu na magĩtũma andũ aingĩ matuĩke arutwo. Magĩcooka makĩhũndũka magĩthiĩ Lisitera, na Ikonia, na Antiokia,
English: When they had preached the Good News to that city and had made many disciples, they returned to Lystra, Iconium, and Antioch,

Kikuyu: magĩĩkagĩra arutwo hinya na kũmomĩrĩria nĩgeetha matũũre marũmĩtie wĩtĩkio. Makameeraga atĩrĩ, “No nginya ithuĩ tũgerere mathĩĩna-inĩ maingĩ nĩguo tũtoonye ũthamaki-inĩ wa Ngai.”
English: strengthening the souls of the disciples, exhorting them to continue in the faith, and that through many afflictions we must enter into God’s Kingdom.

Kikuyu: Paũlũ na Baranaba makĩmaamũrĩra athuuri a kanitha na makĩhooya Ngai mehingĩte kũrĩa irio, makĩmaneana kũrĩ Mwathani, o we ũrĩa meehokete.
English: When they had appointed elders for them in every assembly, and had prayed with fasting, they commended them to the Lord on whom they had believed.

Kikuyu: Thuutha wa gũtuĩkanĩria Pisidia, magĩkinya Pamufilia,
English: They passed through Pisidia and came to Pamphylia.

Kikuyu: na maarĩkia kũhunjia kiugo kũu Periga, magĩikũrũka magĩthiĩ Atalia.
English: When they had spoken the word in Perga, they went down to Attalia.

Kikuyu: Moima Atalia makĩhaica marikabu, magĩcooka Antiokia, o kũrĩa maahoeirwo wega wa Ngai mbere nĩ ũndũ wa wĩra ũrĩa maarĩkĩtie kũruta.
English: From there they sailed to Antioch, from where they had been committed to the grace of God for the work which they had fulfilled.

Kikuyu: Maakinya kũu magĩcookanĩrĩria andũ a kanitha hamwe, makĩmeera ũrĩa wothe Ngai ekĩte agereire harĩo na ũrĩa aahingũrĩire andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ mũrango wa gwĩtĩkia.
English: When they had arrived and had gathered the assembly together, they reported all the things that God had done with them, and that he had opened a door of faith to the nations.

Kikuyu: Nao magĩikara na arutwo kũu ihinda iraaya.
English: They stayed there with the disciples for a long time.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, andũ amwe magĩikũrũka kuuma Judea magĩthiĩ Antiokia, na nĩmarutaga ariũ a Ithe witũ makameera atĩrĩ: “Mũngĩaga kũrua kũringana na mũtugo ũrĩa warutanirwo nĩ Musa, mũtingĩhonoka.”
English: Some men came down from Judea and taught the brothers,† “Unless you are circumcised after the custom of Moses, you can’t be saved.”

Kikuyu: Ũndũ ũyũ ũgĩtũma Paũlũ na Baranaba macookanĩrie na makararanie mũno nao.
English: Therefore when Paul and Barnabas had no small discord and discussion with them, they appointed Paul, Barnabas, and some others of them to go up to Jerusalem to the apostles and elders about this question.

Kikuyu: Naguo kanitha ũkĩmoimagaria, na rĩrĩa maatuĩkanagĩria Foinike na Samaria, nĩmaheanaga ũhoro wa ũrĩa andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ maagarũrũkire kũrĩ Ngai. Ũhoro ũcio ũgĩkenia ariũ a Ithe witũ mũno.
English: They, being sent on their way by the assembly, passed through both Phoenicia and Samaria, declaring the conversion of the Gentiles. They caused great joy to all the brothers.

Kikuyu: Na maakinya Jerusalemu, makĩamũkĩrwo nĩ kanitha, na atũmwo, na athuuri, nao makĩmeera maũndũ mothe marĩa Ngai eekĩte agereire harĩo.
English: When they had come to Jerusalem, they were received by the assembly and the apostles and the elders, and they reported everything that God had done with them.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo andũ amwe a arĩa meetĩkĩtie a thiritũ ya Afarisai makĩrũgama, makiuga atĩrĩ, “Andũ a Ndũrĩrĩ no nginya marue na maathwo atĩ marũmagie watho wa Musa.”
English: But some of the sect of the Pharisees who believed rose up, saying, “It is necessary to circumcise them, and to command them to keep the law of Moses.”

Kikuyu: Nao atũmwo na athuuri magĩcemania nĩgeetha maarĩrĩrie ũhoro ũcio.
English: The apostles and the elders were gathered together to see about this matter.

Kikuyu: Thuutha wa mĩario mĩingĩ, Petero akĩrũgama, akĩmarĩria, akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ, nĩmũũĩ atĩ ihinda rĩhĩtũku, Ngai nĩathuurire kuuma gatagatĩ kanyu nĩguo andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ maigue ndũmĩrĩri ya Ũhoro- ũrĩa-Mwega kuuma kũrĩ niĩ na nĩguo metĩkie.
English: When there had been much discussion, Peter rose up and said to them, “Brothers, you know that a good while ago God made a choice among you that by my mouth the nations should hear the word of the Good News and believe.

Kikuyu: Nake Ngai, ũrĩa ũũĩ ngoro, akĩonania atĩ nĩametĩkĩrĩte na ũndũ wa kũmahe Roho Mũtheru, o ta ũrĩa aatũhete.
English: God, who knows the heart, testified about them, giving them the Holy Spirit, just like he did to us.

Kikuyu: Ndeekĩrire ngũũrani gatagatĩ gaitũ nao, nĩgũkorwo maatheririe ngoro ciao na ũndũ wa wĩtĩkio.
English: He made no distinction between us and them, cleansing their hearts by faith.

Kikuyu: Hakĩrĩ ũguo-rĩ, nĩ kĩĩ kĩratũma mwende kũgeria Ngai na ũndũ wa gwĩkĩra arutwo icooki ngingo rĩrĩa ithuĩ o na kana maithe maitũ tũtarĩ twahota gũkuua?
English: Now therefore why do you tempt God, that you should put a yoke on the neck of the disciples which neither our fathers nor we were able to bear?

Kikuyu: Aca! Twĩtĩkĩtie atĩ tũhonokete nĩ ũndũ wa wega wa Mwathani witũ Jesũ, o ta ũrĩa o nao mahonokete.”
English: But we believe that we are saved through the grace of the Lord Jesus,‡ just as they are.”

Kikuyu: Nakĩo kĩũngano gĩothe gĩgĩkira ki gĩgĩthikĩrĩria Baranaba na Paũlũ makĩheana ũhoro wa ciama na morirũ iria Ngai eekĩte kũrĩ andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ agereire harĩo.
English: All the multitude kept silence, and they listened to Barnabas and Paul reporting what signs and wonders God had done among the nations through them.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa maarĩkirie kwaria, Jakubu akĩaria akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ, ta thikĩrĩriai.
English: After they were silent, James answered, “Brothers, listen to me.

Kikuyu: Simoni nĩatũtaarĩria ũrĩa Ngai oonanirie wendo wake hĩndĩ ya mbere na ũndũ wa gwĩthuurĩra andũ kuuma kũrĩ andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ nĩguo matuĩke aake.
English: Simeon has reported how God first visited the nations to take out of them a people for his name.

Kikuyu: Nacio ciugo cia anabii ikaringana na ũhoro ũcio, o ta ũrĩa kwandĩkĩtwo atĩrĩ:
English: This agrees with the words of the prophets. As it is written,

Kikuyu: “ ‘Thuutha wa ũguo nĩngacooka, na njake rĩngĩ hema ya Daudi ĩrĩa ĩgwĩte. Nĩngaka iganjo rĩayo rĩngĩ, na ndĩmĩcookie harĩa yarĩ,
English: ‘After these things I will return. I will again build the tabernacle of David, which has fallen. I will again build its ruins. I will set it up

Kikuyu: nĩgeetha matigari marongoragie Mwathani, o na andũ othe a Ndũrĩrĩ arĩa metanagio na rĩĩtwa rĩakwa, ũguo nĩguo Mwathani ũrĩa wĩkaga maũndũ macio ekuuga’
English: that the rest of men may seek after the Lord: all the Gentiles who are called by my name, says the Lord, who does all these things.’*

Kikuyu: marĩa moĩkaine kuuma o tene.
English: “All of God’s works are known to him from eternity.

Kikuyu: “Nĩ ũndũ ũcio-rĩ, niĩ ngũtua atĩrĩ, tũtige kũritũhĩria ũhoro andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ arĩa maragarũrũka kũrĩ Ngai.
English: Therefore my judgment is that we don’t trouble those from among the Gentiles who turn to God,

Kikuyu: Handũ ha ũguo-rĩ, twagĩrĩirwo tũmaandĩkĩre marũa, tũmeere metheemage kuumana na irio iria ithaahĩtio nĩ mĩhianano, ningĩ metheemage kuumana na ũmaraya, na kuumana na nyama cia nyamũ cia gũitwo, o na kuumana na thakame.
English: but that we write to them that they abstain from the pollution of idols, from sexual immorality, from what is strangled, and from blood.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo ũhoro wa Musa nĩwahunjagio matũũra- inĩ mothe o kuuma mahinda ma tene mũno, na nĩũthomagwo thunagogi-inĩ mĩthenya yothe ya Thabatũ.”
English: For Moses from generations of old has in every city those who preach him, being read in the synagogues every Sabbath.”

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo atũmwo na athuuri, hamwe na kanitha wothe, magĩtua itua mathuure andũ amwe ao mamatũme Antiokia marĩ na Paũlũ na Baranaba. Magĩthuura Judasi (ũrĩa wetagwo Barasaba) na Sila, nao andũ aya eerĩ maarĩ atongoria thĩinĩ wa ariũ a Ithe witũ.
English: Then it seemed good to the apostles and the elders, with the whole assembly, to choose men out of their company, and send them to Antioch with Paul and Barnabas: Judas called Barsabbas, and Silas, chief men among the brothers.§

Kikuyu: Makĩmatũma na marũa maya: Nĩ ithuĩ atũmwo na athuuri, o ithuĩ ariũ a Ithe wanyu, Kũrĩ andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ arĩa metĩkĩtie kũu Antiokia, na Suriata, na Kilikia: Amũkĩrai ngeithi.
English: They wrote these things by their hand: “The apostles, the elders, and the brothers, to the brothers who are of the Gentiles in Antioch, Syria, and Cilicia: greetings.

Kikuyu: Nĩtũiguĩte atĩ andũ amwe nĩmooimire kũrĩ ithuĩ matarĩ na rũũtha rwitũ, na makĩmũthĩĩnia, na makĩnyariira meciiria manyu nĩ ũndũ wa maũndũ marĩa maaririe.
English: Because we have heard that some who went out from us have troubled you with words, unsettling your souls, saying, ‘You must be circumcised and keep the law,’ to whom we gave no commandment;

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, ithuothe nĩtũiguanĩire tũthuure andũ amwe tũmatũme kũrĩ inyuĩ marĩ na arata aitũ endwa, Baranaba na Paũlũ,
English: it seemed good to us, having come to one accord, to choose out men and send them to you with our beloved Barnabas and Paul,

Kikuyu: andũ arĩa matwarĩrĩirie mĩoyo yao ũgwati-inĩ nĩ ũndũ wa rĩĩtwa rĩa Mwathani witũ Jesũ Kristũ.
English: men who have risked their lives for the name of our Lord Jesus Christ.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio nĩtwatũma Judasi na Sila nĩgeetha makindĩre ũhoro ũyũ tũramwandĩkĩra
English: We have sent therefore Judas and Silas, who themselves will also tell you the same things by word of mouth.

Kikuyu: Nĩkuonekete arĩ wega kũrĩ Roho Mũtheru o na kũrĩ ithuĩ twage kũmũkuuithia mũrigo ũkĩrĩte maũndũ maya:
English: For it seemed good to the Holy Spirit, and to us, to lay no greater burden on you than these necessary things:

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, mũtikarĩĩage irio iria irutĩirwo mĩhianano, na mũtikanyuuage thakame, na mũtikarĩĩage nyama cia nyamũ cia gũitwo, na mũtigane na ũmaraya. Mwetheema maũndũ macio-rĩ, nĩmũgwĩka wega. Tigwoi ũhoro.
English: that you abstain from things sacrificed to idols, from blood, from things strangled, and from sexual immorality, from which if you keep yourselves, it will be well with you. Farewell.”

Kikuyu: Nao andũ acio makiugĩrwo ũhoro, na makĩharũrũka magĩthiĩ Antiokia, kũrĩa maacookanĩrĩirie kanitha hamwe, na makĩneana marũa macio.
English: So, when they were sent off, they came to Antioch. Having gathered the multitude together, they delivered the letter.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa andũ maathomire marũa macio, magĩkena nĩ ũndũ wa ndũmĩrĩri ya kũmoomĩrĩria ĩrĩa yaandĩkĩtwo marũa-inĩ macio.
English: When they had read it, they rejoiced over the encouragement.

Kikuyu: Nao Judasi na Sila, arĩa o ene maarĩ anabii, makĩaria maũndũ maingĩ ma kũũmĩrĩria na kuongerera ariũ a Ithe witũ hinya.
English: Judas and Silas, also being prophets themselves, encouraged the brothers with many words and strengthened them.

Kikuyu: Thuutha wa gũikara kũu kwa ihinda-rĩ, makiugĩrwo ũhoro na irathimo cia thayũ nĩ ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ, nĩguo macooke kũrĩ andũ arĩa maamatũmĩte.
English: After they had spent some time there, they were dismissed in peace from the brothers to the apostles.

Kikuyu: (Nowe Sila akĩona arĩ wega aikare kũu.)
No Paũlũ na Baranaba magĩtigwo kũu Antiokia, kũrĩa o, na andũ angĩ aingĩ maarutanaga na makahunjia kiugo kĩa Mwathani.
English: But Paul and Barnabas stayed in Antioch, teaching and preaching the word of the Lord, with many others also.

Kikuyu: Thuutha wa mĩthenya ĩigana ũna, Paũlũ akĩĩra Baranaba atĩrĩ, “Reke tũcooke tũgaceerere ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ matũũra-inĩ mothe marĩa tũhunjĩtie kiugo kĩa Mwathani, tũkamenye ũrĩa matariĩ.”
English: After some days Paul said to Barnabas, “Let’s return now and visit our brothers in every city in which we proclaimed the word of the Lord, to see how they are doing.”

Kikuyu: Baranaba nĩeendaga kuoya Johana, ũrĩa wetagwo Mariko mathiĩ nake,
English: Barnabas planned to take John, who was called Mark, with them also.

Kikuyu: no Paũlũ ndonaga arĩ wega mathiĩ nake, tondũ nĩamatiganĩirie rĩrĩa maarĩ Pamufilia na akĩaga gũthiĩ kũruta wĩra nao.
English: But Paul didn’t think that it was a good idea to take with them someone who had withdrawn from them in Pamphylia, and didn’t go with them to do the work.

Kikuyu: Makĩgĩa na gũkararania kũnene o nginya makĩamũkana. Baranaba akĩoya Mariko, makĩhaica marikabu, magĩthiĩ Kuporo,
English: Then the contention grew so sharp that they separated from each other. Barnabas took Mark with him and sailed away to Cyprus,

Kikuyu: no Paũlũ agĩthuura Sila, na maarĩkia kũhooerwo wega wa Mwathani nĩ ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ, magĩthiĩ.
English: but Paul chose Silas and went out, being commended by the brothers to the grace of God.

Kikuyu: Agĩtuĩkanĩria Suriata na Kilikia, agĩĩkagĩra makanitha hinya.
English: He went through Syria and Cilicia, strengthening the assemblies.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, Paũlũ agĩkinya Deribe agĩcooka akiuma kuo agĩthiĩ Lisitera, kũrĩa mũrutwo wetagwo Timotheo aatũũraga, ũrĩa nyina aarĩ Mũyahudi mũtumia wetĩkĩtie Ngai, no ithe aarĩ Mũyunani.
English: He came to Derbe and Lystra; and behold, a certain disciple was there, named Timothy, the son of a Jewess who believed, but his father was a Greek.

Kikuyu: Ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ a kũu Lisitera na Ikonia nĩmaragia wega igũrũ rĩake.
English: The brothers who were at Lystra and Iconium gave a good testimony about him.

Kikuyu: Paũlũ nĩendaga mathiĩ nake rũgendo, no nĩ tondũ wa Ayahudi arĩa maatũũraga kũu, akĩmũruithia nĩgũkorwo nĩmooĩ atĩ ithe aarĩ Mũyunani.
English: Paul wanted to have him go out with him, and he took and circumcised him because of the Jews who were in those parts, for they all knew that his father was a Greek.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa maathiiaga kuuma itũũra nginya rĩrĩa rĩngĩ, nĩmeeraga andũ matua marĩa maatuĩtwo nĩ atũmwo na athuuri kũu Jerusalemu nĩguo andũ mamaathĩkagĩre.
English: As they went on their way through the cities, they delivered the decrees to them to keep which had been ordained by the

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio makanitha makĩgĩa na hinya ũhoro-inĩ wa gwĩtĩkia na makongererekaga mũigana o mũthenya.
English: So the assemblies were strengthened in the faith, and increased in number daily.

Kikuyu: Paũlũ na andũ arĩa maarĩ nao magĩtuĩkanĩria bũrũri wothe wa Firigia na Galatia, nĩgũkorwo nĩmagirĩtio nĩ Roho Mũtheru kũhunjia kiugo kũu bũrũri wa Asia.
English: When they had gone through the region of Phrygia and Galatia, they were forbidden by the Holy Spirit to speak the word in Asia.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa maakinyire mũhaka-inĩ wa Misia, makĩgeria gũtoonya Bithinia, no Roho wa Jesũ ndaigana kũmetĩkĩria gũtoonya.
English: When they had come opposite Mysia, they tried to go into Bithynia, but the Spirit didn’t allow them.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio makĩhĩtũka Misia, magĩikũrũka magĩkinya Teroa.
English: Passing by Mysia, they came down to Troas.

Kikuyu: Ũtukũ ũcio-rĩ, Paũlũ akĩona kĩoneki kĩa mũndũ wa Makedonia arũgamĩte akĩmũthaitha, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Ũka gũkũ Makedonia ũtũteithie.”
English: A vision appeared to Paul in the night. There was a man of Macedonia standing, begging him and saying, “Come over into Macedonia and help us.”

Kikuyu: Paũlũ aarĩkia kuona kĩoneki kĩu, tũkĩĩhaarĩria o hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩgeetha tũthiĩ Makedonia, tũgĩtua atĩ Ngai nĩatwĩtĩte tũkamahunjĩrie Ũhoro-ũrĩa-Mwega.
English: When he had seen the vision, immediately we sought to go out to Macedonia, concluding that the Lord had called us to preach the Good News to them.

Kikuyu: Twaarĩkia kuuma Teroa, tũgĩthiĩ na marikabu twerekeire Samotherake, na mũthenya ũyũ ũngĩ tũgĩthiĩ Neapoli.
English: Setting sail therefore from Troas, we made a straight course to Samothrace, and the day following to Neapolis;

Kikuyu: Kuuma kũu tũgĩthiĩ Filipi, kũrĩa gwathagwo nĩ andũ a Roma, na nĩrĩo rĩarĩ itũũra rĩrĩa inene rĩa bũrũri ũcio wa Makedonia. Na tũgĩikara kũu mĩthenya ĩtaarĩ mĩingĩ.
English: and from there to Philippi, which is a city of Macedonia, the foremost of the district, a Roman colony. We were staying some days in this city.

Kikuyu: Mũthenya wa Thabatũ wakinya tũkiuma nja ya kĩhingo gĩa itũũra, tũgĩthiĩ rũũĩ-inĩ, tũtaanyĩte kuona handũ ha kũhooerwo Ngai. Tũgĩikara thĩ na tũkĩambĩrĩria kwarĩria andũ-a-nja arĩa monganĩte hau.
English: On the Sabbath day we went outside of the city by a riverside, where we supposed there was a place of prayer, and we sat down and spoke to the women who had come together.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, ũmwe wa andũ-a-nja acio maathikĩrĩirie eetagwo Lidia, ũrĩa warĩ mwonjoria wa nguo cia rangi wa ndathi, na oimĩte itũũra rĩa Thuatira, na aarĩ mũhooi Ngai. Nake Mwathani akĩhingũra
English: A certain woman named Lydia, a seller of purple, of the city of Thyatira, one who worshiped God, heard us. The Lord opened her heart to listen to the things which were spoken by Paul.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa we na andũ a nyũmba yake maabatithirio-rĩ, nĩatũnyiitire ũgeni gwake mũciĩ agĩtwĩra atĩrĩ, “Angĩkorwo nĩmuonete atĩ nĩnjĩtĩkĩtie Mwathani-rĩ, ũkai mũgaikare nyũmba gwakwa.” Nake agĩtũringĩrĩria tũthiĩ.
English: When she and her household were baptized, she begged us, saying, “If you have judged me to be faithful to the Lord, come into my house and stay.” So she persuaded us.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩmwe tũgĩthiĩ harĩa haahooyagĩrwo Ngai, nĩtwatũngirwo nĩ mũirĩtu warĩ ngombo na nĩarĩ na roho wa kũragũra. Nake nĩatũmaga ene we meonere mbeeca nyingĩ mũno nĩ ũndũ wa ũragũri wake
English: As we were going to prayer, a certain girl having a spirit of divination met us, who brought her masters much gain by fortune telling.

Kikuyu: Mũirĩtu ũcio akĩrũmĩrĩra Paũlũ hamwe na ithuĩ, akĩanagĩrĩra atĩrĩ, “Andũ aya nĩ ndungata cia Ngai- Ũrĩa-ũrĩ-Igũrũ-Mũno, na maramũhe ũhoro wa njĩra ya ũrĩa mũngĩhonokio.”
English: Following Paul and us, she cried out, “These men are servants of the Most High God, who proclaim to us a way of salvation!”

Kikuyu: Mũirĩtu ũcio agĩthiĩ na mbere na gwĩka ũguo mĩthenya mĩingĩ. Marigĩrĩrio-inĩ Paũlũ agĩthirĩka mũno nginya akĩhũgũkĩra roho ũcio akĩwĩĩra atĩrĩ, “Ndagwatha thĩinĩ wa rĩĩtwa rĩa Jesũ Kristũ uume thĩinĩ wake!” O hĩndĩ ĩyo roho ũcio ũkĩmuuma.
English: She was doing this for many days. But Paul, becoming greatly annoyed, turned and said to the spirit, “I command you in the name of Jesus Christ to come out of her!” It came out that very hour.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa ene mũirĩtu ũcio ngombo maamenyire atĩ mwĩhoko wao wa kuona mbeeca nĩwathira, makĩnyiita Paũlũ na Sila makĩmaguucũrũria makĩmatoonyia ndũnyũ mbere ya aathani.
English: But when her masters saw that the hope of their gain was gone, they seized Paul and Silas and dragged them into the marketplace before the rulers.

Kikuyu: Makĩmarehe mbere ya aciirithania, makiuga atĩrĩ, “Andũ aya nĩ Ayahudi, na nĩmararehe ngũĩ itũũra-inĩ riitũ
English: When they had brought them to the magistrates, they said, “These men, being Jews, are agitating our city

Kikuyu: nĩ ũndũ wa kũrutana mĩtugo ĩrĩa ithuĩ andũ a Roma tũtagĩrĩirwo nĩ gwĩtĩkĩra o na kana kũmĩĩka.”
English: and advocate customs which it is not lawful for us to accept or to observe, being Romans.”

Kikuyu: Nakĩo kĩrĩndĩ gĩgĩũkĩrĩra Paũlũ na Sila kĩmeeke ũũru no aciirithania makĩruta watho atĩ marutwo nguo na mahũũrwo iboko.
English: The multitude rose up together against them and the magistrates tore their clothes from them, then commanded them to be beaten with rods.

Kikuyu: Thuutha wa kũhũũrwo mũno magĩikio njeera, na mũrangĩri wa njeera agĩathwo amarangĩre wega.
English: When they had laid many stripes on them, they threw them into prison, charging the jailer to keep them safely.

Kikuyu: Aarĩkia kwamũkĩra watho ũcio, akĩmaikia kanyũmba ka na thĩinĩ na akĩohithania magũrũ mao na ndungu.
English: Having received such a command, he threw them into the inner prison and secured their feet in the stocks.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, ta ũtukũ gatagatĩ, Paũlũ na Sila nĩkũhooya maahooyaga makĩinagĩra Ngai nyĩmbo, nao andũ arĩa angĩ mohetwo makamathikĩrĩria.
English: But about midnight Paul and Silas were praying and singing hymns to God, and the prisoners were listening to them.

Kikuyu: Na o rĩmwe gũkĩgĩa gĩthingithia kĩnene o nginya mĩthingi ya njeera ĩgĩthingitha. Nayo mĩrango yothe ya njeera ikĩhingũka o rĩmwe, na mĩnyororo ya mũndũ o mũndũ ĩkĩregera.
English: Suddenly there was a great earthquake, so that the foundations of the prison were shaken; and immediately all the doors were opened, and everyone’s bonds were loosened.

Kikuyu: Mũrangĩri wa njeera akĩarahũka, na rĩrĩa aarorire akĩona mĩrango ya njeera ĩrĩ mĩhingũku, akĩruta rũhiũ rwake rwa njora akĩenda kwĩyũraga tondũ eeciiririe atĩ andũ arĩa moohetwo nĩmoorĩte.*
English: The jailer, being roused out of sleep and seeing the prison doors open, drew his sword and was about to kill himself, supposing that the prisoners had escaped.

Kikuyu: No Paũlũ akĩanĩrĩra, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Ndũkeegere ngero! Ithuothe tũrĩ ho!”
English: But Paul cried with a loud voice, saying, “Don’t harm yourself, for we are all here!”

Kikuyu: Nake mũrangĩri ũcio wa njeera agĩĩtia matawa, agĩcooka akĩhanyũka thĩinĩ akĩinainaga, akĩĩgũithia mbere ya Paũlũ na Sila.
English: He called for lights, sprang in, fell down trembling before Paul and Silas,

Kikuyu: Agĩcooka akĩmoimia na nja, akĩũria atĩrĩ, “Athuuri aya, njagĩrĩirwo nĩ gwĩka atĩa nĩguo honoke?”
English: brought them out, and said, “Sirs, what must I do to be saved?”

Kikuyu: Nao makĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Ĩtĩkia Mwathani Jesũ, na nĩũkũhonoka, wee, o hamwe na andũ a nyũmba yaku.”
English: They said, “Believe in the Lord Jesus Christ, and you will be saved, you and your household.”

Kikuyu: Magĩcooka makĩmwarĩria ũhoro wa kiugo kĩa Mwathani, hamwe na andũ arĩa angĩ othe maarĩ gwake nyũmba.
English: They spoke the word of the Lord to him, and to all who were in his house.

Kikuyu: O ihinda rĩu rĩa ũtukũ, mũrangĩri ũcio wa njeera akĩmooya, akĩmathambia ironda, na o hĩndĩ ĩyo we na andũ othe a nyũmba yake makĩbatithio.
English: He took them the same hour of the night and washed their stripes, and was immediately baptized, he and all his household.

Kikuyu: Mũrangĩri ũcio wa njeera akĩmatwara gwake nyũmba, na akĩmahe irio. Akĩiyũrwo nĩ gĩkeno tondũ nĩeetĩkirie Ngai, we o hamwe na andũ a nyũmba yake yothe.
English: He brought them up into his house and set food before them, and rejoiced greatly with all his household, having believed in God.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, gwakĩa aciirithania magĩtũma thigari ikeere mũrangĩri ũcio wa njeera atĩrĩ, “Rekereria andũ acio mathiĩ.”
English: But when it was day, the magistrates sent the sergeants, saying, “Let those men go.”

Kikuyu: Nake mũrangĩri ũcio wa njeera akĩĩra Paũlũ atĩrĩ, “Aciirithania maathana, moiga atĩ wee na Sila mũrekererio. Rĩu no mũthiĩ. Thiĩi na thayũ.”
English: The jailer reported these words to Paul, saying, “The magistrates have sent to let you go; now therefore come out and go in peace.”

Kikuyu: No Paũlũ akĩĩra thigari icio atĩrĩ, “Maratũhũũrire mbere ya mũingĩ tũtaciirithĩtio, o na gũtuĩka ithuĩ tũrĩ raiya a Roma, na maratũikia njeera. Rĩu marakĩenda gũtũruta na hitho? Aca! Reke mooke o ene matũrute nja, matuumagarie.”
English: But Paul said to them, “They have beaten us publicly without a trial, men who are Romans, and have cast us into prison! Do they now release us secretly? No, most certainly, but let them come themselves and bring us out!”

Kikuyu: Nacio thigari igĩcookeria aciirithania ũhoro ũcio, na rĩrĩa maaiguire atĩ Paũlũ na Sila maarĩ raiyaa Roma, makĩmaka.
