English: The sergeants reported these words to the magistrates, and they were afraid when they heard that they were Romans,

Kikuyu: Nao magĩũka kũmahooreria, na makĩmoimagaria kuuma njeera, makĩmathaitha moime itũũra rĩu mathiĩ.
English: and they came and begged them. When they had brought them out, they asked them to depart from the city.

Kikuyu: Paũlũ na Sila maarĩkia kuuma njeera, magĩthiĩ mũciĩ kwa Lidia, kũrĩa maakorire ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ, na makĩmoomĩrĩria. Magĩcooka magĩĩthiĩra. 17
English: They went out of the prison and entered into Lydia’s house. When they had seen the brothers, they encouraged them, then departed.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, maarĩkia gũtuĩkanĩria Amufipoli na Apolonia, magĩkinya Thesalonike, kũrĩa kwarĩ na thunagogi ya Ayahudi.
English: Now when they had passed through Amphipolis and Apollonia, they came to Thessalonica, where there was a Jewish synagogue.

Kikuyu: Paũlũ agĩtoonya thunagogi, o ta ũrĩa aamenyerete, na mĩthenya ĩtatũ ya Thabatũ akĩaranĩria nao kuuma Maandĩko-inĩ,
English: Paul, as was his custom, went in to them; and for three Sabbath days reasoned with them from the Scriptures,

Kikuyu: akĩmataarĩria na akĩmaiguithia atĩ kwarĩ o nginya Kristũ anyariirĩke na ariũke kuuma kũrĩ arĩa akuũ. Akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Jesũ ũyũ ndĩramũhe ũhoro wake- rĩ, nĩwe Kristũ.”
English: explaining and demonstrating that the Christ had to suffer and rise again from the dead, and saying, “This Jesus, whom I proclaim to you, is the Christ.”

Kikuyu: Ayahudi amwe magĩtĩkĩra ũhoro ũcio na magĩtuĩka a thiritũ ya Paũlũ na Sila, o ũndũ ũmwe na gĩkundi kĩnene kĩa Ayunani etigĩri-Ngai, o na atumia aingĩ arĩa maarĩ igweta.
English: Some of them were persuaded and joined Paul and Silas: of the devout Greeks a great multitude, and not a few of the chief women.

Kikuyu: No Ayahudi makĩigua ũiru; nĩ ũndũ ũcio magĩcaria andũ arĩa maarĩ mĩtugo mĩũru kuuma ndũnyũ, makĩmacookanĩrĩria gĩkundi, na makĩambĩrĩria kũruta ngũĩ kũu itũũra-inĩ. Nao makĩhanyũka kwa Jasoni gwetha Paũlũ na Sila nĩgeetha mamoimie nja kũrĩ kĩrĩndĩ.
English: But the unpersuaded Jews took along† some wicked men from the marketplace and gathering a crowd, set the city in an uproar. Assaulting the house of Jason, they sought to bring them out to the people.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩa maamaagire, magĩkururia Jasoni na ariũ a Ithe witũ amwe, makĩmarehe kũrĩ anene a itũũra, makĩanagĩrĩra atĩrĩ: “Andũ aya nĩo marehete thĩĩna thĩ yothe, na rĩu nĩmookĩte gũkũ,
English: When they didn’t find them, they dragged Jason and certain brothers‡ before the rulers of the city, crying, “These who have turned the world upside down have come here also,

Kikuyu: na Jasoni nĩamanyiitĩte ũgeni thĩinĩ wa nyũmba yake. Othe nĩmarakararia watho wa Kaisari, makoiga atĩ nĩ kũrĩ na mũthamaki ũngĩ wĩtagwo Jesũ.”
English: whom Jason has received. These all act contrary to the decrees of Caesar, saying that there is another king, Jesus!”

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa maaiguire ũguo, kĩrĩndĩ kĩu na anene a itũũra magĩtangĩka mũno.
English: The multitude and the rulers of the city were troubled when they heard these things.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo magĩĩtia Jasoni na andũ arĩa angĩ maarĩ nao irĩhi rĩa kũmarũgamĩrĩra, magĩcooka makĩmarekereria mathiĩ.
English: When they had taken security from Jason and the rest, they let them go.

Kikuyu: Na kwarĩĩkia gũtuka-rĩ, ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ magĩtũma Paũlũ na Sila mathiĩ Berea. Nao maakinya kuo, magĩtoonya thunagogi ya Ayahudi.
English: The brothers immediately sent Paul and Silas away by night to Beroea. When they arrived, they went into the Jewish synagogue.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, Ayahudi a Berea maarĩ a ngoro njega gũkĩra Ayahudi a Thesalonike, nĩgũkorwo nĩmamũkĩrire ndũmĩrĩri marĩ na wendo mũingĩ, na magathuthuuragia Maandĩko o mũthenya, nĩgeetha moone kana ũrĩa Paũlũ aameeraga warĩ ũhoro wa ma.
English: Now these were more noble than those in Thessalonica, in that they received the word with all readiness of mind, examining the Scriptures daily to see whether these things were so.

Kikuyu: Ayahudi aingĩ nĩmetĩkirie, o ũndũ ũmwe na atumia aingĩ a Ayunani arĩa maarĩ igweta, o na andũ arũme aingĩ Ayunani.
English: Many of them therefore believed; also of the prominent Greek women, and not a few men.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Ayahudi a Thesalonike maamenyire atĩ Paũlũ nĩahunjagia kiugo kĩa Ngai kũu Berea-rĩ, magĩthiĩ kũu o nakuo, magĩthogotha kĩrĩndĩ na magĩgĩthũkia ngoro.
English: But when the Jews of Thessalonica had knowledge that the word of God was proclaimed by Paul at Beroea also, they came there likewise, agitating the multitudes.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ o ĩyo ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ magĩtũma Paũlũ athiĩ ndwere-inĩ cia iria, no Sila na Timotheo magĩtigwo kũu Berea.
English: Then the brothers immediately sent out Paul to go as far as to the sea, and Silas and Timothy still stayed there.

Kikuyu: Nao andũ arĩa moimagaririe Paũlũ, makĩmũkinyia Athene, na magĩcooka marĩ na ndũmĩrĩri ya Sila na Timotheo atĩ mathiĩ kũrĩ Paũlũ narua o ta ũrĩa kũngĩhoteka.
English: But those who escorted Paul brought him as far as Athens. Receiving a commandment to Silas and Timothy that they should come to him very quickly, they departed.

Kikuyu: Paũlũ o ametereire kũu Athene-rĩ, nĩatangĩkire ngoro mũno nĩkuona ũrĩa itũũra rĩu rĩaiyũrĩte mĩhianano.
English: Now while Paul waited for them at Athens, his spirit was provoked within him as he saw the city full of idols.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio nĩaranagĩria na Ayahudi o na Ayunani arĩa meeyamũrĩire Ngai kũu thunagogi-inĩ, o na ningĩ akaaragia ndũnyũ-inĩ o mũthenya na andũ arĩa maakoragwo ho.
English: So he reasoned in the synagogue with the Jews and the devout persons, and in the marketplace every day with those who met him.

Kikuyu: Nakĩo gĩkundi kĩa Aepikurio na Asitoiko, arutani a ũũgĩ,* makĩambĩrĩria gũkararania nake. Amwe ao makĩũria atĩrĩ, “Mũndũ ũyũ wa mũhuhu arageria kuuga atĩa?” Nao andũ arĩa angĩ makiuga atĩrĩ, “Nĩ ta mũndũ ũrahunjia ũhoro wa ngai ngʼeni.” Moigaga ũguo tondũ Paũlũ aahunjagia Ũhoro Mwega wa Jesũ o na wa kũriũka gwake.
English: Some of the Epicurean and Stoic philosophers also§ were conversing with him. Some said, “What does this babbler want to say?” Others said, “He seems to be advocating foreign deities,” because he preached Jesus and the resurrection.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo makĩmuoya na makĩmũtwara mbere ya mũcemanio wa Areopago,† makĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “No twende kũmenya atĩrĩ, ũrutani ũyũ mwerũ ũrarutana nĩ ũrĩkũ?
English: They took hold of him and brought him to the Areopagus, saying, “May we know what this new teaching is, which you are speaking about?

Kikuyu: Tũraigua ũgĩtwarĩria maũndũ mageni, na nĩtũkwenda kũmenya ũrĩa moigĩte.”
English: For you bring certain strange things to our ears. We want to know therefore what these things mean.”

Kikuyu: (Andũ othe a Athene na andũ a kũngĩ arĩa maatũũraga kuo matirĩ ũndũ ũngĩ meekaga tiga kwaria na gũthikĩrĩria ũhoro wa maũndũ marĩa mageni).
English: Now all the Athenians and the strangers living there spent their time in nothing else, but either to tell or to hear some new thing.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo Paũlũ akĩrũgama mũcemanio-inĩ wa kĩama kĩu gĩetagwo Areopago akĩmeera atĩrĩ: “Andũ aya a Athene, nĩnguona atĩ maũndũ-inĩ mothe mũrĩ andũ a ndini mũno.
English: Paul stood in the middle of the Areopagus and said, “You men of Athens, I perceive that you are very religious in all things.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo o na nĩnyonete kĩgongona kĩandĩkĩtwo maandĩko maya: KŨRĨ NGAI ĨRĨA ĨTOĨO, rĩrĩa ngoretwo ngĩceera na ngarora wega indo cianyu iria nyamũre cia kũhooywo. Na rĩrĩ, kĩrĩa mũhooyaga mũtooĩ nĩkĩo ngũmũhe ũhoro wakĩo.
English: For as I passed along and observed the objects of your worship, I also found an altar with this inscription: ‘TO AN UNKNOWN GOD.’ What therefore you worship in ignorance, I announce to you.

Kikuyu: “Ngai ũrĩa wombire thĩ na indo ciothe iria irĩ kuo nĩwe Mwathani wa igũrũ na thĩ, na ndatũũraga hekarũ ciakĩtwo na moko.
English: The God who made the world and all things in it, he, being Lord of heaven and earth, doesn’t dwell in temples made with hands.

Kikuyu: Na ndatungatagwo na moko ma andũ, ta abataire kĩndũ, tondũ we mwene nĩwe ũheaga andũ muoyo na mĩhũmũ o na indo iria ingĩ ciothe.
English: He isn’t served by men’s hands, as though he needed anything, seeing he himself gives to all life and breath and all things.

Kikuyu: Kuuma harĩ mũndũ ũmwe,
English: He made from one blood every nation of men to dwell on all the surface of the earth, having determined appointed seasons and the boundaries of their dwellings,

Kikuyu: Ngai eekire ũguo nĩgeetha andũ mamũmaathe na hihi mamũhambatĩrie mamuone, o na gũtuĩka ndarĩ haraaya na o ũmwe witũ.
English: that they should seek the Lord, if perhaps they might reach out for him and find him, though he is not far from each one of us.

Kikuyu: ‘Nĩgũkorwo nĩ thĩinĩ wake tũtũũraga na tũgetwara, na tũgakorwo tũrĩ muoyo.’ O ta ũrĩa aandĩki amwe a nyĩmbo cianyu moigĩte atĩrĩ, ‘Ithuĩ tũrĩ a rũciaro rwake.’
English: ‘For in him we live, move, and have our being.’ As some of your own poets have said, ‘For we are also his offspring.’

Kikuyu: “Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, kuona atĩ tũrĩ a rũciaro rwa Ngai, tũtiagĩrĩirwo nĩ gwĩciiria atĩ Ngai ahaana ta mũhianano wa thahabu kana wa betha, o na kana wa ihiga, ũrĩa ũthondeketwo na mũthugundĩre na ũũgĩ wa mũndũ.
English: Being then the offspring of God, we ought not to think that the Divine Nature is like gold, or silver, or stone, engraved by art and design of man.

Kikuyu: Mahinda ma tene Ngai ndaigana kũrũmbũiya ũhoro ũcio wa kwaga ũmenyo, no rĩu nĩathĩte andũ othe a kũndũ guothe merire.
English: The times of ignorance therefore God overlooked. But now he commands that all people everywhere should repent,

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo nĩatuĩte mũthenya ũrĩa agaatuĩra thĩ ciira na kĩhooto na ũndũ wa mũndũ ũrĩa athuurĩte. Nĩamenyithanĩtie ũndũ ũyũ kũrĩ andũ othe na ũndũ wa kũriũkia Jesũ kuuma kũrĩ arĩa akuũ.”
English: because he has appointed a day in which he will judge the world in righteousness by the man whom he has ordained; of which he has given assurance to all men, in that he has raised him from the dead.”

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa maiguire ũhoro wa kũriũka kwa arĩa akuũ, amwe ao magĩtheka, no angĩ makiuga atĩrĩ, “Nĩtũkwenda gũkũigua ũkĩaria ũhoro ũcio hĩndĩ ĩngĩ.”
English: Now when they heard of the resurrection of the dead, some mocked; but others said, “We want to hear you again concerning this.”

Kikuyu: Maarĩkia kuuga ũguo, Paũlũ akĩehera Kĩama- inĩ kĩu.
English: Thus Paul went out from among them.

Kikuyu: Andũ mataarĩ aingĩ magĩtuĩka arũmĩrĩri a Paũlũ na magĩĩtĩkia. Ũmwe wao aarĩ Dionisio, mũndũ warĩ wa thiritũ ya Areopago, o na mũtumia wetagwo Damari, na angĩ maigana ũna.
English: But certain men joined with him and believed, including Dionysius the Areopagite, and a woman named Damaris, and others with them.

Kikuyu: Thuutha ũcio, Paũlũ akiuma Athene agĩthiĩ Korinitho.
English: After these things Paul departed from Athens and came to Corinth.

Kikuyu: Aakinya kũu agĩkora Mũyahudi wetagwo Akula, mũndũ waciarĩirwo Ponto, na aakoretwo oka kuuma Italia marĩ na mũtumia wake Pirisila, tondũ Kilaudio nĩathanĩte Ayahudi othe moime Roma. Nake Paũlũ agĩthiĩ kũmona,
English: He found a certain Jew named Aquila, a man of Pontus by race, who had recently come from Italy with his wife Priscilla, because Claudius had commanded all the Jews to depart from Rome. He came to them,

Kikuyu: na tondũ Paũlũ aarĩ mũtumi wa hema o tao-rĩ, agĩikarania na akĩrutithania wĩra nao.
English: and because he practiced the same trade, he lived with them and worked, for by trade they were tent makers.

Kikuyu: O mũthenya wa Thabatũ nĩaheanaga ũhoro thunagogi-inĩ, akĩgeria kũiguithia Ayahudi na Ayunani.
English: He reasoned in the synagogue every Sabbath and persuaded Jews and Greeks.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Sila na Timotheo mookire kuuma Makedonia, Paũlũ akĩĩrutĩra gũkorwo akĩhunjia mahinda mothe, na akoimbũragĩra Ayahudi atĩ Jesũ nĩwe Kristũ.
English: When Silas and Timothy came down from Macedonia, Paul was compelled by the Spirit, testifying to the Jews that Jesus was the Christ.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩa Ayahudi maakararirie Paũlũ na makĩmũruma, akĩribariba nguo ciake nĩ ũndũ wa kũngʼũrĩka na akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Thakame yanyu ĩromũcookerera! Niĩ ndirĩ ũndũ ingĩũrio nĩ ũndũ wanyu. Kuuma rĩu ngũcooka gũthiĩ kũrĩ andũ- a-Ndũrĩrĩ.”
English: When they opposed him and blasphemed, he shook out his clothing and said to them, “Your blood be on your own heads! I am clean. From now on, I will go to the Gentiles!”

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo Paũlũ akiuma kũu thunagogi-inĩ, agĩthiĩ kwa mũndũ wetagwo Titio Jusito, warĩ mũhooi Ngai, nayo nyũmba yake yariganĩtie na thunagogi.
English: He departed there and went into the house of a certain man named Justus, one who worshiped God, whose house was next door to the synagogue.

Kikuyu: Nake Kirisipo, ũrĩa warĩ mũtongoria wa thunagogi, agĩĩtĩkia Mwathani hamwe na nyũmba yake yothe; na andũ aingĩ a Korinitho arĩa maamũiguire akĩaria magĩĩtĩkia na makĩbatithio.
English: Crispus, the ruler of the synagogue, believed in the Lord with all his house. Many of the Corinthians, when they heard, believed and were baptized.

Kikuyu: Ũtukũ ũmwe Mwathani akĩĩra Paũlũ na kĩoneki atĩrĩ, “Ndũkae gwĩtigĩra; thiĩ na mbere na kũheana ũhoro, na ndũgaakire.
English: The Lord said to Paul in the night by a vision, “Don’t be afraid, but speak and don’t be silent;

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo ndĩ hamwe nawe, na gũtirĩ mũndũ ũgũgũtharĩkĩra agwĩke ũũru, tondũ ndĩ na andũ aingĩ gũkũ itũũra-inĩ rĩĩrĩ.”
English: for I am with you, and no one will attack you to harm you, for I have many people in this city.”

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Paũlũ agĩikara kũu mwaka ũmwe na nuthu, akĩmarutaga kiugo kĩa Ngai.
English: He lived there a year and six months, teaching the word of God among them.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Galio aarĩ barũthi wa Akaia, Ayahudi makĩnyiitana magĩtharĩkĩra Paũlũ na makĩmũtwara igooti-inĩ.
English: But when Gallio was proconsul of Achaia, the Jews with one accord rose up against Paul and brought him before the judgment seat,

Kikuyu: Makiuga atĩrĩ, “Mũndũ ũyũ araringĩrĩria andũ kũhooya Ngai na njĩra ĩrĩa ĩreganĩte na watho.”
English: saying, “This man persuades men to worship God contrary to the law.”

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa Paũlũ endaga kwaria, Galio akĩĩra Ayahudi atĩrĩ, “Korwo inyuĩ Ayahudi mũrateta nĩ ũndũ wa ũndũ mũũru kana ngero njũru-rĩ, nĩ hangĩrĩ na gĩtũmi gĩa kũmũthikĩrĩria inyuĩ.
English: But when Paul was about to open his mouth, Gallio said to the Jews, “If indeed it were a matter of wrong or of wicked crime, you Jews, it would be reasonable that I should bear with you;

Kikuyu: No kuona atĩ nĩ ũhoro wa ciũria ikoniĩ ciugo na marĩĩtwa na watho wanyu inyuĩ ene-rĩ, menyai ũrĩa mũgwĩka. Niĩ ndigũtuĩka mũtui ciira wa maũndũ ta macio.”
English: but if they are questions about words and names and your own law, look to it yourselves. For I don’t want to be a judge of these matters.”

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio akiuga marutũrũrwo kuumaigooti-inĩ.
English: So he drove them from the judgment seat.

Kikuyu: HĩndĩĩyoAyahudiacioothemakĩgarũrũkĩraSosithenemũtongoriawathunagogi, na makĩmũhũũrĩra hau mbere ya igooti. No Galio ndaigana kũrũmbũyania na ũhoro ũcio o na atĩa.
English: Then all the Greeks seized Sosthenes, the ruler of the synagogue, and beat him before the judgment seat. Gallio didn’t care about any of these things.

Kikuyu: Paũlũ nĩaikarire kũu Korinitho kwa ihinda. Agĩcooka agĩtiga ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ kuo, akĩhaica marikabu agĩthiĩ Suriata, marĩ hamwe na Pirisila na Akula. Atanahaica marikabu, aambire akĩenjwo njuĩrĩ yake arĩ kũu Kenikirea, tondũ wa mwĩhĩtwa eehĩtĩte.
English: Paul, having stayed after this many more days, took his leave of the brothers,† and sailed from there for Syria, together with Priscilla and Aquila. He shaved his head in Cenchreae, for he had a vow.

Kikuyu: Nao maakinya Efeso, Paũlũ agĩtiga Pirisila na Akula kuo. We mwene agĩtoonya thunagogi na akĩaranĩria na Ayahudi.
English: He came to Ephesus, and he left them there; but he himself entered into the synagogue and reasoned with the Jews.

Kikuyu: Nao makĩmũũria aikaranganie nao kuo, nowe akĩrega.
English: When they asked him to stay with them a longer time, he declined;

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩa oimaga kuo, akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Aakorwo nĩ wendi wa
English: but taking his leave of them, he said, “I must by all means keep this coming feast in Jerusalem, but I will return again to you if God wills.” Then he set sail from Ephesus.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa aakinyire Kaisarea, akĩambata agĩthiĩ kũgeithia Kanitha, agĩcooka agĩikũrũka agĩthiĩ nginya Antiokia.
English: When he had landed at Caesarea, he went up and greeted the assembly, and went down to Antioch.

Kikuyu: Na thuutha wa Paũlũ gũikaranga kũu Antiokia, akiuma kuo, na agĩthiĩ kũndũ na kũndũ bũrũri wothe wa Galatia na Firigia, akĩũmagĩrĩria arutwo othe.
English: Having spent some time there, he departed and went through the region of Galatia and Phrygia, in order, establishing all the disciples.

Kikuyu: O ihinda-inĩ rĩu, Mũyahudi wetagwo Apolo, waciarĩirwo Alekisanderia,* agĩũka Efeso. Aarĩ mũndũmũthomu, naaarĩnaũũgĩmũingĩmũnowaMaandĩko.
English: Now a certain Jew named Apollos, an Alexandrian by race, an eloquent man, came to Ephesus. He was mighty in the Scriptures.

Kikuyu: NĩarutĩtwonanjĩraĩrĩayaMwathani, na akaaragia na kĩyo kĩnene, na akarutanaga ũhoro wa Jesũ atekũhĩtia, o na gũtuĩka ũbatithio ũrĩa ooĩ warĩ o wa Johana.
English: This man had been instructed in the way of the Lord; and being fervent in spirit, he spoke and taught accurately the things concerning Jesus, although he knew only the baptism of John.

Kikuyu: Nake akĩambĩrĩria kwaria na ũcamba kũu thunagogi-inĩ. Rĩrĩa Pirisila na Akula maamũiguire akĩaria, makĩmũnyiita ũgeni kwao mũciĩ na makĩmũtaarĩria ũhoro wa njĩra ya Ngai wega makĩria.
English: He began to speak boldly in the synagogue. But when Priscilla and Aquila heard him, they took him aside, and explained to him the way of God more accurately.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa Apolo eendaga gũthiĩ Akaia, ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ makĩmũũmĩrĩria na makĩandĩkĩra arutwo akuo marũa makĩmeera mamwamũkĩre. Na aakinya kuo agĩtuĩka ũteithio mũnene kũrĩ andũ arĩa meetĩkĩtie nĩ ũndũ wa wega wa Ngai.
English: When he had determined to pass over into Achaia, the brothers encouraged him; and wrote to the disciples to receive him. When he had come, he greatly helped those who had believed through grace;

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo arĩ na ũhoti mũingĩ, nĩahootire Ayahudi biũ mbere ya andũ othe, akĩonanagia kuuma kũrĩ Maandĩko atĩ Jesũ nĩwe Kristũ.
English: for he powerfully refuted the Jews, publicly showing by the Scriptures that Jesus was the Christ.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, hĩndĩ ĩyo Apolo arĩ kũu Korinitho, Paũlũ akĩgerera njĩra ĩrĩa yatuĩkanĩirie mwena wa rũgongo agĩthiĩ nginya Efeso. Aakinya kũu agĩkora arutwo amwe kuo.
English: While Apollos was at Corinth, Paul, having passed through the upper country, came to Ephesus and found certain disciples.

Kikuyu: Akĩmooria atĩrĩ, “Nĩmwamũkĩrire Roho Mũtheru rĩrĩa mwetĩkirie?” Nao makĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Aca, ithuĩ o na tũtirĩ twaigua atĩ nĩ kũrĩ Roho Mũtheru.”
English: He said to them, “Did you receive the Holy Spirit when you believed?” They said to him, “No, we haven’t even heard that there is a Holy Spirit.”

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Paũlũ akĩmooria atĩrĩ, “Mwakĩbatithirio ũbatithio ũrĩkũ?” Nao makĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Twabatithirio ũbatithio wa Johana.”
English: He said, “Into what then were you baptized?” They said, “Into John’s baptism.”

Kikuyu: Paũlũ akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Ũbatithio wa Johana warĩ ũbatithio wa kwĩrira kwa mehia. Eeraga andũ metĩkie ũrĩa ũgũũka thuutha wake, nake nĩwe Jesũ.”
English: Paul said, “John indeed baptized with the baptism of repentance, saying to the people that they should believe in the one who would come after him, that is, in Christ Jesus.”†

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa maaiguire ũguo, makĩbatithio thĩinĩ wa rĩĩtwa rĩa Mwathani Jesũ.
English: When they heard this, they were baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa Paũlũ aamaigĩrĩire moko, Roho Mũtheru akĩmaikũrũkĩra, nao makĩaria na thiomi ingĩ na makĩratha mohoro.
English: When Paul had laid his hands on them, the Holy Spirit came on them and they spoke with other languages and prophesied.

Kikuyu: Nao othe maarĩ ta andũ ikũmi na eerĩ.
English: They were about twelve men in all.

Kikuyu: Paũlũ agĩtoonya thunagogi, na ihinda rĩa mĩeri ĩtatũ akĩaria na ũcamba kuo, akĩgeragia kũringĩrĩria andũ ũhoro wa ũthamaki wa Ngai.
English: He entered into the synagogue and spoke boldly for a period of three months, reasoning and persuading about the things concerning God’s Kingdom.

Kikuyu: No amwe ao makĩũmia ngoro; makĩaga gwĩtĩkia na magĩcambia Njĩra ĩyo mbere ya kĩrĩndĩ. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Paũlũ akĩmeherera. Akĩoya arutwo agĩthiĩ nao nyũmba ĩrĩa yathomithagĩrio andũ ya Turano, akaaranagĩria na andũ o mũthenya arĩ kuo.
English: But when some were hardened and disobedient, speaking evil of the Way before the multitude, he departed from them and separated the disciples, reasoning daily in the school of Tyrannus.

Kikuyu: Ũndũ ũyũ nĩwathiire na mbere ihinda ta rĩa mĩaka ĩĩrĩ, o nginya Ayahudi na Ayunani othe arĩa maatũũraga bũrũri wa Asia makĩigua kiugo kĩa Mwathani.
English: This continued for two years, so that all those who lived in Asia heard the word of the Lord Jesus, both Jews and Greeks.

Kikuyu: Ngai nĩaringire ciama cia mwanya agereire harĩ Paũlũ,
English: God worked special miracles by the hands of Paul,

Kikuyu: o nginya itambaya na nguo iria ciahutagia mwĩrĩ wake igatwarĩrwo andũ arĩa maarĩ arũaru, nao makahona mĩrimũ yao na ngoma thũku ikamatiga.
English: so that even handkerchiefs or aprons were carried away from his body to the sick, and the diseases departed from them, and the evil spirits went out.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, Ayahudi amwe arĩa maathiiaga makĩingataga ngoma thũku makĩgeria kũhũthĩra rĩĩtwa rĩa Mwathani Jesũ harĩ andũ arĩa maarĩ na ndaimono. Moigaga atĩrĩ, “Thĩinĩ wa rĩĩtwa rĩa Jesũ, ũrĩa ũhunjagio nĩ Paũlũ, ndagwatha uume.”
English: But some of the itinerant Jews, exorcists, took on themselves to invoke over those who had the evil spirits the name of the Lord Jesus, saying, “We adjure you by Jesus whom Paul preaches.”

Kikuyu: Ariũ mũgwanja a Mũyahudi wetagwo Sikeva, warĩ mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai mũnene, nĩmekaga ũguo.
English: There were seven sons of one Sceva, a Jewish chief priest, who did this.

Kikuyu: Mũthenya ũmwe, ngoma thũku ĩkĩmacookeria ĩkĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Jesũ nĩndĩmũũĩ na nĩnjũũĩ ũhoro wa Paũlũ, no inyuĩ mũrĩ a?”
English: The evil spirit answered, “Jesus I know, and Paul I know, but who are you?”

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo mũndũ ũcio warĩ na ngoma thũku akĩmarũgĩrĩra akĩmatooria othe. Akĩmahũũra mũno nginya makiuma nyũmba ĩyo makĩũra marĩ njaga makiuraga thakame.
English: The man in whom the evil spirit was leaped on them, overpowered them, and prevailed against them, so that they fled out of that house naked and wounded.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Ayahudi na Ayunani arĩa maatũũraga Efeso maamenyire ũhoro ũcio, othe makĩiyũrwo nĩ guoya, narĩo rĩĩtwa rĩa Mwathani Jesũ rĩgĩtũũgĩrio.
English: This became known to all, both Jews and Greeks, who lived at Ephesus. Fear fell on them all, and the name of the Lord Jesus was magnified.

Kikuyu: Andũ aingĩ a arĩa meetĩkirie makĩyumĩria na makiumbũra waganu wao matekũhitha.
English: Many also of those who had believed came, confessing and declaring their deeds.

Kikuyu: Andũ aingĩ arĩa maaragũraga, magĩcookanĩrĩria mabuku mao na makĩmacinĩra mbere ya andũ othe. Rĩrĩa maatarire thogora wa mabuku macio, magĩkinyia durakima ngiri mĩrongo ĩtano.
English: Many of those who practiced magical arts brought their books together and burned them in the sight of all. They counted their price, and found it to be fifty thousand pieces of silver.‡

Kikuyu: Kiugo kĩa Mwathani gĩkĩhunja mũno na njĩra ĩyo na gĩkĩgĩa na hinya.
English: So the word of the Lord was growing and becoming mighty.

Kikuyu: Thuutha wa maũndũ macio mothe gwĩkĩka, Paũlũ agĩtua itua rĩa gũthiĩ Jerusalemu, atuĩkanĩirie Makedonia na Akaia. Akiuga atĩrĩ, “Ndaarĩkia gũkinya kũu, no nginya ngaacooka thiĩ Roma.”
English: Now after these things had ended, Paul determined in the Spirit, when he had passed through Macedonia and Achaia, to go to Jerusalem, saying, “After I have been there, I must also see Rome.”

Kikuyu: Agĩtũma andũ eerĩ a arĩa maamũteithagĩrĩria, na nĩo Timotheo na Erasito, mathiĩ Makedonia, nake agĩikaranga kũu bũrũri wa Asia gwa kahinda kanini.
English: Having sent into Macedonia two of those who served him, Timothy and Erastus, he himself stayed in Asia for a while.

Kikuyu: Ihinda-inĩ rĩu nĩ kwagĩire na thogothogo nene ĩkoniĩ ũhoro wa Njĩra ĩyo.
English: About that time there arose no small disturbance concerning the Way.

Kikuyu: Mũturi wa indo cia betha wetagwo Demeterio, ũrĩa wathondekaga tũmĩhianano twa betha twa Aritemi, nĩatũmaga andũ arĩa maaturaga indo icio magĩe na wonjoria mũnene.
English: For a certain man named Demetrius, a silversmith who made silver shrines of Artemis, brought no little business to the craftsmen,

Kikuyu: Nake agĩcookanĩrĩria andũ acio maaturaga indo icio, hamwe na aruti a wĩra arĩa maarutaga wĩra ũhaanaine na wao, akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Andũ aya, inyuĩ nĩmũũĩ nĩtuonaga uumithio mũnene kuuma kũrĩ wonjoria ũyũ.
English: whom he gathered together with the workmen of like occupation, and said, “Sirs, you know that by this business we have our wealth.

Kikuyu: Na nĩmũrona na mũkaigua ũrĩa mũndũ ũyũ ũretwo Paũlũ aiguithĩtie na akahĩtithia andũ aingĩ gũkũ Efeso, na makĩria bũrũri wothe wa Asia akiugaga atĩ ngai iria ithondeketwo nĩ mũndũ ti ngai o na hanini.
English: You see and hear that not at Ephesus alone, but almost throughout all Asia, this Paul has persuaded and turned away many people, saying that they are no gods that are made with hands.

Kikuyu: Ũgwati ũrĩa ũrĩ ho nĩ atĩ to wonjoria witũ wiki ũkũmenererio, no o na hekarũ ya ngai ya mũndũ-wa-nja ĩrĩa nene ĩĩtagwo Aritemi nĩĩkwagithio kĩene, na ngai ĩyo ya mũndũ-wa-nja yo nyene, ĩrĩa ĩhooyagwo bũrũri-inĩ wothe wa Asia, na thĩ yothe, nĩĩkũimwo ũkaru wayo.”
English: Not only is there danger that this our trade come into disrepute, but also that the temple of the great goddess Artemis will be counted as nothing and her majesty destroyed, whom all Asia and the world worships.”

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa maaiguire ũguo, makĩngʼũrĩka mũno na makĩanĩrĩra, makiugaga atĩrĩ: “Aritemi wa Aefeso nĩ mũnene!”
English: When they heard this they were filled with anger, and cried out, saying, “Great is Artemis of the Ephesians!”

Kikuyu: Na thuutha wa kahinda kanini itũũra rĩu rĩkĩiyũra inegene. Andũ acio othe makĩnyiita Gayo na Arisitariko arĩa moimĩte Makedonia na Paũlũ, nao makĩhanyũka marĩ ngoro ĩmwe magĩtoonya nyũmba ĩrĩa yagomanagwo nĩ ũndũ wa kuona mĩago.
English: The whole city was filled with confusion, and they rushed with one accord into the theater, having seized Gaius and Aristarchus, men of Macedonia, Paul’s companions in travel.

Kikuyu: Paũlũ nĩendaga kwĩyumĩria mbere ya kĩrĩndĩ kĩu, no arutwo makĩmũgiria.
English: When Paul wanted to enter in to the people, the disciples didn’t allow him.

Kikuyu: O na anene amwe a bũrũri wa Asia arĩa maarĩ arata a Paũlũ makĩmũtũmanĩra, makĩmũthaitha ndakagerie gũtoonya nyũmba ĩyo yagomanagwo nĩ ũndũ wa kuona mĩago.
English: Certain also of the Asiarchs, being his friends, sent to him and begged him not to venture into the theater.

Kikuyu: Kĩũngano kĩu gĩkĩaga ũiguano: Amwe maanagĩrĩra makoiga ũũ, na arĩa angĩ makoiga ũũ. Andũ arĩa aingĩ o na matiamenyaga gĩtũmi gĩa gũkorwo hau.
English: Some therefore cried one thing, and some another, for the assembly was in confusion. Most of them didn’t know why they had come together.

Kikuyu: Ayahudi makiumĩria Alekisanda mbere ya kĩrĩndĩ, na andũ amwe a kĩrĩndĩ kĩu makĩanĩrĩra makĩmwĩra ũrĩa egwĩka. Nake akĩmakiria na moko nĩgeetha eyarĩrĩrie mbere yao.
English: They brought Alexander out of the multitude, the Jews putting him forward. Alexander beckoned with his hand, and would have made a defense to the people.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩa maamenyire atĩ aarĩ Mũyahudi, othe makĩanĩrĩra na mũgambo ũmwe ihinda ta rĩa mathaa meerĩ makiugaga atĩrĩ, “Aritemi wa Aefeso nĩ mũnene!”
English: But when they perceived that he was a Jew, all with one voice for a time of about two hours cried out, “Great is Artemis of the Ephesians!”

Kikuyu: Karani wa kĩama gĩa itũũra rĩu agĩkiria kĩrĩndĩ kĩu, agĩcooka akĩmeera atĩrĩ: “Andũ aya a Efeso, githĩ thĩ yothe ndĩũĩ atĩ itũũra rĩa Efeso nĩrĩo rĩene hekarũ ĩrĩa ĩtũũragwo nĩ Aritemi ũrĩa mũnene, na mũhianano wake ũrĩa waharũrũkire kuuma igũrũ?
English: When the town clerk had quieted the multitude, he said, “You men of Ephesus, what man is there who doesn’t know that the city of the Ephesians is temple keeper of the great goddess Artemis, and of the image which fell down from Zeus?

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, kuona atĩ maũndũ macio matingĩkaanĩka-rĩ, mwagĩrĩirwo nĩ gũkira, na mũtikae gwĩka ũndũ wa ihenya.
English: Seeing then that these things can’t be denied, you ought to be quiet and to do nothing rash.

Kikuyu: Inyuĩ mwarehe andũ aya haha, o na gũtuĩka ti kũiya maiyĩte indo cia hekarũ, kana makaruma ngai iitũ ya mũndũ-wa-nja.
English: For you have brought these men here, who are neither robbers of temples nor blasphemers of your goddess.

Kikuyu: Hakĩrĩ ũguo-rĩ, angĩkorwo Demeterio na andũ acio angĩ marutaga wĩra nake marĩ na mateta igũrũ rĩa mũndũ o wothe, maciirĩro nĩmahingũre na aciirithania marĩ ho. No mamathitange.
English: If therefore Demetrius and the craftsmen who are with him have a matter against anyone, the courts are open and there are proconsuls. Let them press charges against one another.

Kikuyu: Angĩkorwo harĩ na ũndũ ũngĩ mũngĩenda kuuga makĩria ma ũguo, ũcio no ũciirĩirwo kĩama-inĩ kĩa watho.
English: But if you seek anything about other matters, it will be settled in the regular assembly.

Kikuyu: Ũrĩa kũrĩrĩunĩatĩ, tũrĩũgwati-inĩwagũthitangĩrwongũĩĩnoyaũmũthĩ. Nakũrĩũguotũtingĩhotakũheana ũhoro wa thogothogo ĩno, tondũ hatirĩ na gĩtũmi kĩa yo.”
English: For indeed we are in danger of being accused concerning today’s riot, there being no cause. Concerning it, we wouldn’t be able to give an account of this commotion.”

Kikuyu: Aarĩkia kuuga ũguo, akĩĩra kĩũngano kĩu gĩthiĩ, akĩniina mũcemanio.
English: When he had thus spoken, he dismissed the assembly.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, ngũĩ ĩyo yarĩkia gũthira, Paũlũ agĩtũmanĩra arutwo, na aarĩkia kũmomĩrĩria, akĩmoigĩra ũhoro agĩthiĩ Makedonia.
English: After the uproar had ceased, Paul sent for the disciples, took leave of them, and departed to go into Macedonia.

Kikuyu: Agĩtuĩkanĩria mĩena ya kuo, akĩaragia ciugo nyingĩ cia kũũmĩrĩria andũ, na marigĩrĩrio agĩkinya bũrũri wa Ũyunani,
English: When he had gone through those parts and had encouraged them with many words, he came into Greece.

Kikuyu: kũrĩa aaikarire mĩeri ĩtatũ. Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa Paũlũ eeharagĩria kũhaica marikabu athiĩ Suriata, agĩtua itua gũcookera Makedonia, tondũ Ayahudi nĩmaciirĩire kũmwĩka ũũru.
English: When he had spent three months there, and a plot was made against him by Jews as he was about to set sail for Syria, he determined to return through Macedonia.

Kikuyu: Paũlũ aathiire marĩ hamwe na Sopatiro mũrũ wa Puro, mũndũ wa kuuma Berea, na Arisitariko, na Sekundo, andũ a kuuma Thesalonike, na Gayo wa kuuma Deribe, o hamwe na Timotheo, ningĩ na Tikiko, na Tirofimo, andũ a kuuma bũrũri wa Asia.
English: These accompanied him as far as Asia: Sopater of Beroea, Aristarchus and Secundus of the Thessalonians, Gaius of Derbe, Timothy, and Tychicus and Trophimus of Asia.

Kikuyu: Andũ acio magĩthiĩ mbere magĩtweterera kũu Teroa.
English: But these had gone ahead, and were waiting for us at Troas.

Kikuyu: No ithuĩ tũkĩhaica marikabu, tũkiuma Filipi thuutha wa Gĩathĩ kĩa Mĩgate
English: We sailed away from Philippi after the days of Unleavened Bread, and came to them at Troas in five days, where we stayed seven days.

Kikuyu: Mũthenya wa mbere wa kiumia nĩtwagomanire hamwe nĩguo twenyũre mũgate. Paũlũ akĩarĩria andũ, na tondũ nĩatanyĩte gũthiĩ mũthenya ũcio warũmĩrĩire-rĩ, agĩthiĩ na mbere kwaria nginya ũtukũ gatagatĩ.
English: On the first day of the week, when the disciples were gathered together to break bread, Paul talked with them, intending to depart on the next day; and continued his speech until midnight.

Kikuyu: Nĩ kwarĩ na matawa maingĩ maakanaga kũu nyũmba ya igũrũ kũrĩa twagomanĩte.
English: There were many lights in the upper room where we† were gathered together.

Kikuyu: Nake mwanake wetagwo Eutiko aikarĩte ndirica-inĩ, na o ũrĩa Paũlũ aathiaga na mbere na kwaria- rĩ, noguo mwanake ũcio akĩragĩrĩria gũtoorio nĩ toro. Rĩrĩa aakomire, akĩgũa thĩ kuuma ngoroba ya gatatũ nao mamuoya magĩkora nĩ mũkuũ.
English: A certain young man named Eutychus sat in the window, weighed down with deep sleep. As Paul spoke still longer, being weighed down by his sleep, he fell down from the third floor and was taken up dead.

Kikuyu: Paũlũ agĩikũrũka, agĩkomera mwanake ũcio akĩmũkumbatĩria akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Tigai kũmaka, arĩ muoyo!”
English: Paul went down and fell upon him, and embracing him said, “Don’t be troubled, for his life is in him.”

Kikuyu: Agĩcooka akĩambata nyũmba ya igũrũ, akĩenyũra mũgate na akĩrĩa. Na thuutha wa kũmaarĩria nginya gũgĩkĩa-rĩ, akiuma kũu, agĩthiĩ.
English: When he had gone up, had broken bread and eaten, and had talked with them a long while, even until break of day, he departed.

Kikuyu: Nao andũ makĩinũkia mwanake ũcio arĩ muoyo, na mahooreire ngoro mũno.
English: They brought the boy in alive, and were greatly comforted.

Kikuyu: No ithuĩ tũgĩthiĩ mbere tũgĩtoonya marikabu tũgĩthiĩ nginya Aso, kũrĩa twathiire tũkĩoyaga Paũlũ. Aathondekete mũbango ũyũ mbere tondũ we athiĩte kũu magũrũ.
English: But we, going ahead to the ship, set sail for Assos, intending to take Paul aboard there; for he had so arranged, intending himself to go by land.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa aatũtũngire kũu Aso, tũkĩmũkuua na marikabu, tũgĩthiĩ nginya Mitilene.
English: When he met us at Assos, we took him aboard and came to Mitylene.

Kikuyu: Mũthenya ũcio ũngĩ warũmĩrĩire tũkĩhaica marikabu, tũgĩthiĩ tũgĩkinya harĩa haaringaine na Kio, na thuutha wa mũthenya ũcio tũgĩkinya Samo, naguo mũthenya ũcio ũngĩ warũmĩrĩire, tũgĩkinya Mileto.
English: Sailing from there, we came the following day opposite Chios. The next day we touched at Samos and stayed at Trogyllium, and the day after we came to Miletus.

Kikuyu: Paũlũ nĩatuĩte itua rĩa gũthiĩ ahĩtũkĩte Efeso na marikabu nĩguo ndagate mahinda kũu bũrũri wa Asia, nĩ ũndũ nĩehĩkaga nĩguo kwahoteka akinye Jerusalemu, mũthenya wa Bendegothito.
English: For Paul had determined to sail past Ephesus, that he might not have to spend time in Asia; for he was hastening, if it were possible for him, to be in Jerusalem on the day of Pentecost.

Kikuyu: Paũlũ arĩ kũu Mileto, agĩtũmana Efeso, agĩĩta athuuri a kanitha moke harĩ we.
English: From Miletus he sent to Ephesus and called to himself the elders of the assembly.

Kikuyu: Nao maakinya, akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Inyuĩ nĩ mũũĩ ũrĩa ndaatũũraga ihinda rĩrĩa rĩothe ndaaikarire na inyuĩ, kuuma o mũthenya wa mbere ũrĩa ndookire bũrũri wa Asia.
English: When they had come to him, he said to them, “You yourselves know, from the first day that I set foot in Asia, how I was with you all the time,

Kikuyu: Nĩndatungatĩire Mwathani ndĩnyiihĩtie mũno na ndĩ na maithori, o na gũtuĩka nĩndageririo mũno nĩ ũndũ Ayahudi nĩmaathugundaga kũnjĩka ũũru.
English: serving the Lord with all humility, with many tears, and with trials which happened to me by the plots of the Jews;

Kikuyu: Inyuĩ nĩmũũĩ atĩ ndirĩ ndatithia kũhunjia ũndũ o wothe ũngĩmũguna no ndĩmũrutagĩra mbere ya andũ othe na ngathiiaga o nyũmba o nyũmba.
English: how I didn’t shrink from declaring to you anything that was profitable, teaching you publicly and from house to house,

Kikuyu: Na ngoimbũrĩra Ayahudi o hamwe na Ayunani atĩ no nginya magarũrũke kũrĩ Ngai na ũndũ wa kwĩrira na magĩe na wĩtĩkio thĩinĩ wa Mwathani witũ Jesũ.
English: testifying both to Jews and to Greeks repentance toward God and faith toward our Lord Jesus.‡

Kikuyu: “Na rĩrĩ, nĩ ũndũ wa kũringĩrĩrio nĩ Roho Mũtheru, ndĩrathiĩ Jerusalemu itekũmenya maũndũ marĩa makaangora ndĩ kũu.
English: Now, behold, I go bound by the Spirit to Jerusalem, not knowing what will happen to me there;

Kikuyu: Ũndũ ũrĩa njũũĩ tu nĩ atĩ, Roho Mũtheru nĩandaaraga atĩ matũũra-inĩ mothe, njeera na mathĩĩna nĩcio injetereire.
English: except that the Holy Spirit testifies in every city, saying that bonds and afflictions wait for me.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, ndirona gũtũũra muoyo kũrĩ na bata harĩ niĩ, korwo no ndĩĩkie ihenya na ndĩĩkie wĩra ũrĩa Mwathani Jesũ aaheete, na nĩguo wĩra wa kuumbũra Ũhoro-ũrĩa-Mwega wa wega wa Ngai.
English: But these things don’t count; nor do I hold my life dear to myself, so that I may finish my race with joy, and the ministry which I received from the Lord Jesus, to fully testify to the Good News of the grace of God.

Kikuyu: “Na rĩu nĩnjũũĩ gũtirĩ o na ũmwe thĩinĩ wanyu wa andũ arĩa ndanathiĩ ngĩhunjagĩria ũhoro wa ũthamaki ũgacooka kũnyona rĩngĩ.
English: “Now, behold, I know that you all, among whom I went about preaching God’s Kingdom, will see my face no more.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, ngũmwĩra ũmũthĩ atĩ gũtirĩ thakame ya mũndũ o na ũmwe ngoorio.
English: Therefore I testify to you today that I am clean from the blood of all men,

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ niĩ ndirĩ ndatithia kũmũhunjĩria wendi wa Ngai ũrĩ wothe.
English: for I didn’t shrink from declaring to you the whole counsel of God.

Kikuyu: Mwĩmenyererei inyuĩ ene, na mũmenyerere rũũru ruothe rũrĩa Roho Mũtheru amũtuĩte arori aruo. Tuĩkai arĩithi a kanitha wa Ngai, ũrĩa aagũrire na thakame yake mwene.
English: Take heed, therefore, to yourselves and to all the flock, in which the Holy Spirit has made you overseers, to shepherd the assembly of the Lord and§ God which he purchased with his own blood.

Kikuyu: Nĩnjũũĩ atĩ ndaarĩkia gũthiĩ, njũũi ndĩĩani nĩigooka gatagatĩ-inĩ kanyu na itigacaaĩra rũũru rũrũ.
English: For I know that after my departure, vicious wolves will enter in among you, not sparing the flock.

Kikuyu: O na ningĩ kuuma harĩ inyuĩ nĩgũkoimĩra andũ, na mogomie ũhoro-wa-ma nĩgeetha maguucĩrĩrie arutwo mamarũmĩrĩre.
English: Men will arise from among your own selves, speaking perverse things, to draw away the disciples after them.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio ikaragai mwĩhũgĩte! Ririkanai atĩ ihinda rĩa mĩaka ĩtatũ ndirĩ ndatigithĩria gutaara o ũmwe wanyu ũtukũ na mũthenya ngĩitaga maithori.
English: Therefore watch, remembering that for a period of three years I didn’t cease to admonish everyone night and day with tears.

Kikuyu: “Na rĩu ngũmũneana kũrĩ Ngai o na kũrĩ ũhoro wa wega wake, ũrĩa ũngĩhota kũmwaka wega mũrũme na mũgĩe na igai hamwe na andũ arĩa othe matheretio.
English: Now, brothers,† I entrust you to God and to the word of his grace, which is able to build up and to give you the inheritance among all those who are sanctified.

Kikuyu: Niĩ ndirĩ ndacuumĩkĩra betha kana thahabu, o na kana nguo ya mũndũ o wothe.
English: I coveted no one’s silver, gold, or clothing.

Kikuyu: Inyuĩ ene nĩmũũĩ atĩ ndaarutaga wĩra na moko makwa nĩ ũndũ wa mabata makwa niĩ mwene, na mabata ma andũ arĩa ndakoragwo nao.
English: You yourselves know that these hands served my necessities, and those who were with me.

Kikuyu: Maũndũ- inĩ marĩa mothe ndeekire, nĩndamuonirie atĩ no nginya tũteithagie andũ arĩa matarĩ hinya, na ũndũ wa kũruta wĩra mũritũ ta ũcio, tũkĩririkanaga ciugo iria ciaririo nĩ Mwathani Jesũ we mwene rĩrĩa oigire atĩrĩ, ‘Kũheana nĩ kũrĩ irathimo nyingĩ gũkĩra kũheo.’ ”
English: In all things I gave you an example, that so laboring you ought to help the weak, and to remember the words of the Lord Jesus, that he himself said, ‘It is more blessed to give than to receive.’ ”

Kikuyu: Aarĩkia kuuga ũguo, agĩturia ndu hamwe nao othe makĩhooya.
English: When he had spoken these things, he knelt down and prayed with them all.

Kikuyu: Nao othe makĩrĩra makĩmũhĩm- bagĩria na makĩmũmumunya.
English: They all wept freely, and fell on Paul’s neck and kissed him,

Kikuyu: Ũndũ ũrĩa wamekĩrire kĩeha mũno makĩria, nĩ ũrĩa aameerire atĩ matigacooka kũmuona ũthiũ rĩngĩ. Magĩcooka makĩmumagaria nginya marikabu-inĩ.
English: sorrowing most of all because of the word which he had spoken, that they should see his face no more. Then they accompanied him to the ship.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, thuutha wa gwĩtigithũkania nao, tũkĩhaica marikabu tũgĩthiĩ tũrũngĩrĩirie tũgĩkinya Kosi. Mũthenya ũcio ũngĩ warũmĩrĩire tũgĩkinya Rodo, na twoima kũu tũgĩthiĩ nginya Patara.
English: When we had departed from them and had set sail, we came with a straight course to Cos, and the next day to Rhodes, and from there to Patara.

Kikuyu: Tũgĩkora marikabu yaringaga ĩthiĩ Foinike, na ithuĩ tũkĩmĩhaica tũgĩthiĩ.
English: Having found a ship crossing over to Phoenicia, we went aboard and set sail.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa tuonire Kuporo, tũkĩhĩtũkĩra mwena wa gũthini wakuo, tũgĩthiĩ nginya Suriata. Tũgĩkinya Turo, kũrĩa marikabu iitũ yarutagĩrwo mĩrigo ĩrĩa yarĩ nayo.
English: When we had come in sight of Cyprus, leaving it on the left hand, we sailed to Syria and landed at Tyre, for the ship was there to unload her cargo.

Kikuyu: Tũgĩkora arutwo kuo, na tũgĩikara nao mĩthenya mũgwanja. Nao arutwo acio na ũndũ wa kũmenyithio nĩ Roho Mũtheru magĩthaitha Paũlũ ndagathiĩ Jerusalemu.
English: Having found disciples, we stayed there seven days. These said to Paul through the Spirit that he should not go up to Jerusalem.

Kikuyu: No ihinda riitũ rĩathira, tũkiuma kũu, tũgĩthiĩ na mbere na rũgendo rwitũ. Arutwo othe, hamwe na atumia ao na ciana ciao, magĩtuumagaria, magĩtũkinyia nja ya itũũra, na tũrĩ hau hũgũrũrũ-inĩ cia iria, tũgĩturia ndu, tũkĩhooya Ngai.
English: When those days were over, we departed and went on our journey. They all, with wives and children, brought us on our way until we were out of the city. Kneeling down on the beach, we prayed.

Kikuyu: Twaarĩkia kuganĩra ũhoro, tũkĩhaica marikabu, nao makĩinũka.
English: After saying goodbye to each other, we went on board the ship, and they returned home again.

Kikuyu: Twoima Turo, tũgĩthiĩ na mbere na rũgendo rwitũ, tũgĩkinya Putolemai, tũkĩgeithia ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ kuo na tũgĩikarania nao mũthenya ũmwe.
English: When we had finished the voyage from Tyre, we arrived at Ptolemais. We

Kikuyu: Mũthenya ũyũ ũngĩ tũkiuma kuo, tũgĩthiĩ, tũgĩkinya Kaisarea, na tũgĩikara mũciĩ kwa Filipu ũrĩa warĩ mũhunjia ũmwe wa arĩa Mũgwanja.
English: On the next day, we who were Paul’s companions departed and came to Caesarea. We entered into the house of Philip the evangelist, who was one of the seven, and stayed with him.

Kikuyu: Nake nĩarĩ na airĩtu aake ana matarĩ ahiku, nao nĩmarathaga mohoro.
English: Now this man had four virgin daughters who prophesied.

Kikuyu: Na thuutha wa gũkorwo kuo matukũ maingĩ-rĩ, gũgĩũka mũnabii wetagwo Agabo oimĩte Judea.
English: As we stayed there some days, a certain prophet named Agabus came down from Judea.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa aakinyire harĩ ithuĩ, akĩoya mũcibi wa Paũlũ, akĩwĩĩoha moko na magũrũ, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Roho Mũtheru aroiga atĩrĩ, ‘Ũũ nĩguo Ayahudi a Jerusalemu marĩoha mwene mũcibi ũyũ, na macooke mamũneane kũrĩ andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ.’ ”
English: Coming to us and taking Paul’s belt, he bound his own feet and hands, and said, “The Holy Spirit says: ‘So the Jews at Jerusalem will bind the man who owns this belt, and will deliver him into the hands of the Gentiles.’ ”

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa twaiguire ũguo, ithuĩ na andũ arĩa angĩ maarĩ ho tũgĩthaitha Paũlũ ndakae kwambata Jerusalemu.
English: When we heard these things, both we and the people of that place begged him not to go up to Jerusalem.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo Paũlũ agĩcookia atĩrĩ, “Nĩ kĩĩ kĩratũma mũrĩre, na mũkanjũraga ngoro? To kuohwo gwiki ndĩĩhaarĩirie, no nĩndĩhaarĩirie o na gũkua ndĩ kũu Jerusalemu nĩ ũndũ wa rĩĩtwa rĩa Mwathani Jesũ.”
English: Then Paul answered, “What are you doing, weeping and breaking my heart? For I am ready not only to be bound, but also to die at Jerusalem for the name of the Lord Jesus.”

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa aaregire kũigua ũrĩa twamwĩraga-rĩ, tũgĩtigana nake tũkiuga atĩrĩ, “Wendo wa Mwathani ũrohinga.”
English: When he would not be persuaded, we ceased, saying, “The Lord’s will be done.”

Kikuyu: Thuutha wa ũguo, tũkĩĩhaarĩria tũkĩambata Jerusalemu.
English: After these days we took up our baggage and went up to Jerusalem.

Kikuyu: Arutwo amwe a kuuma Kaisarea magĩthiĩ hamwe na ithuĩ, na magĩtũtwara kwa mũndũ wetagwo Mũnasoni, tũgaikare kuo. Aarĩ mũndũ woimĩte Kuporo, na aarĩ ũmwe wa arutwo arĩa a mbere.
English: Some of the disciples from Caesarea also went with us, bringing one Mnason of Cyprus, an early disciple, with whom we would stay.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa twakinyire Jerusalemu, ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ magĩtwamũkĩra na gĩkeno.
English: When we had come to Jerusalem, the brothers received us gladly.

Kikuyu: Mũthenya ũyũ ũngĩ Paũlũ, na andũ arĩa twarĩ nao tũgĩthiĩ kuona Jakubu, nao athuuri othe maarĩ ho.
English: The day following, Paul went in with us to James; and all the elders were present.

Kikuyu: Nake Paũlũ akĩmageithia na akĩmeera ũrĩa wothe Ngai eekĩire andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ agereire ũtungata-inĩ wake.
English: When he had greeted them, he reported one by one the things which God had worked among the Gentiles through his ministry.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa maaiguire ũguo, makĩgooca Ngai. Magĩcooka makĩĩra Paũlũ atĩrĩ, “Mũrũ wa ithe witũ, nĩwonete ũrĩa Ayahudi ngiri nyingĩ metĩkĩtie, na othe marĩ na kĩyo gĩa kũhingia watho.
English: They, when they heard it, glorified God. They said to him, “You see, brother, how many thousands there are among the Jews of those who have believed, and they are all zealous for the law.

Kikuyu: Nao nĩmamenyithĩtio atĩ nĩ ũrutaga Ayahudi othe arĩa matũũranagia na andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ atĩ magarũrũke matigane na watho wa Musa, ũkĩmeeraga matikaruithie ciana ciao na matigaikare kũringana na mĩtugo iitũ.
English: They have been informed about you, that you teach all the Jews who are among the Gentiles to forsake Moses, telling them not to circumcise their children and not to walk after the customs.

Kikuyu: Tũgwĩka atĩa? Hatirĩ ngaanja nĩmekũigua atĩ nĩũũkĩte.
English: What then? The assembly must certainly meet, for they will hear that you have come.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio ĩka ũrĩa tũgũkwĩra. Tũrĩ na andũ ana mehĩtĩte mwĩhĩtwa.
English: Therefore do what we tell you. We have four men who have taken a vow.

Kikuyu: Thiĩ na andũ aya, ũgwatanĩre nao igongona- inĩ rĩao rĩa gwĩtheria, na ũmarĩhĩre mahũthĩro mao, nĩgeetha menjwo mĩtwe.* Hĩndĩ ĩyo andũ othe nĩmekũmenya atĩ ũhoro ũrĩa maiguĩte ũgũkoniĩ ti wa ma, no atĩ wee mwene nĩwathĩkĩire watho.
English: Take them and purify yourself with them, and pay their expenses for them, that they may shave their heads. Then all will know that there is no truth in the things that they have been informed about you, but that you yourself also walk keeping the law.

Kikuyu: Ha ũhoro wa andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ arĩa metĩkĩtie-rĩ, nĩtwamandĩkĩire, tũkĩmamenyithia itua riitũ, atĩ o metheeme kũrĩa irio iria irutĩirwo mĩhianano, na thakame, na nyama cia nyamũ cia gũitwo, o na ũmaraya.”
English: But concerning the Gentiles who believe, we have written our decision that they should observe no such thing, except that they should keep themselves from food offered to idols, from blood, from strangled things, and from sexual immorality.”

Kikuyu: Mũthenya ũyũ ũngĩ Paũlũ agĩthiĩ na andũ acio, na agĩĩtheria hamwe nao. Agĩcooka agĩthiĩ hekarũ-inĩ akamenyithanie rĩrĩa mĩthenya ya gwĩtheria ĩkaarĩka, na o ũmwe wao arutĩrwo igongona.
English: Then Paul took the men, and the next day purified himself and went with them into the temple, declaring the fulfillment of the days of purification, until the offering was offered for every one of them.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa matukũ macio mũgwanja maakuhĩrĩirie gũthira-rĩ, Ayahudi amwe moimĩte bũrũri wa Asia makĩona Paũlũ hekarũ-inĩ. Magĩthogotha kĩrĩndĩ gĩothe na makĩmũnyiita,
English: When the seven days were almost completed, the Jews from Asia, when they saw him in the temple, stirred up all the multitude and laid hands on him,

Kikuyu: makĩanagĩrĩra atĩrĩ, “Andũ a Isiraeli, tũteithiei! Mũndũ ũyũ nĩ we ũrutaga andũ kũndũ guothe ũhoro wa gũũkĩrĩra andũ aitũ, na watho witũ, na handũ haha. Na hamwe na ũguo-rĩ, nĩarehete Ayunani hekarũ-inĩ, agathaahia handũ haha hatheru.”
English: crying out, “Men of Israel, help! This is the man who teaches all men everywhere against the people, and the law, and this place. Moreover, he also brought Greeks into the temple and has defiled this holy place!”

Kikuyu: (Nĩmonete Tirofimo ũrĩa Mũefeso marĩ na Paũlũ kũu itũũra-inĩ, no magĩĩciiria atĩ Paũlũ nĩamũtoonyetie hekarũ-inĩ.)
English: For they had seen Trophimus the Ephesian with him in the city, and they supposed that Paul had brought him into the temple.

Kikuyu: Narĩo itũũra rĩothe rĩkĩambũrũrũka nao andũ magĩũka mahanyũkĩte moimĩte mĩena yothe. Makĩnyiita Paũlũ, makĩmũguucũrũria makĩmũruta hekarũ, na o hĩndĩ ĩyo ihingo ikĩhingwo.
English: All the city was moved and the people ran together. They seized Paul and dragged him out of the temple. Immediately the doors were shut.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, rĩrĩa maageragia kũmũũraga ũhoro ũgĩkinya harĩ mũnene wa mbũtũ ya thigari cia Roma atĩ itũũra rĩothe rĩa Jerusalemu rĩarĩ na ngũĩ.
English: As they were trying to kill him, news came up to the commanding officer of the regiment that all Jerusalem was in an uproar.

Kikuyu: Nake o rĩmwe akĩoya anene na thigari magĩikũrũka harĩ kĩrĩndĩ matengʼerete. Rĩrĩa andũ acio maarutaga ngũĩ moonire mũnene ũcio na thigari ciake magĩtiga kũhũũra Paũlũ.
English: Immediately he took soldiers and centurions and ran down to them. They,

Kikuyu: Mũnene ũcio wa thigari agĩũka akĩmũnyiita na agĩathana ohwo na mĩnyororo ĩĩrĩ. Agĩcooka akĩũria aarĩ ũ na nĩ atĩa ekĩte.
English: Then the commanding officer came near, arrested him, commanded him to be bound with two chains, and inquired who he was and what he had done.

Kikuyu: Andũ amwe thĩinĩ wa kĩrĩndĩ maanagĩrĩra makoiga ũũ na arĩa angĩ makoiga ũũ; na tondũ mũnene ũcio wa thigari ndangĩamenyire ma ya ũhoro nĩ ũndũ wa mbugĩrĩrio, agĩathana Paũlũ ahingĩrwo nyũmba ya thigari.
English: Some shouted one thing and some another, among the crowd. When he couldn’t find out the truth because of the noise, he commanded him to be brought into the barracks.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa Paũlũ aakinyire ngathĩ-inĩ kĩrĩndĩ kĩu gĩgĩthũka mũno o nginya Paũlũ agĩkuuo nĩ thigari.
English: When he came to the stairs, he was carried by the soldiers because of the violence of the crowd;

Kikuyu: Kĩrĩndĩ kĩrĩa kĩamuumĩte thuutha kĩanagĩrĩra gĩkoiga atĩrĩ, “Mweheriei!”
English: for the multitude of the people followed after, crying out, “Away with him!”

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa thigari ciarĩ o hakuhĩ gũtoonyia Paũlũ nyũmba ya thigari-rĩ, akĩũria mũnene wacio atĩrĩ, “Nĩũkũnjĩtĩkĩria ngwĩre ũndũ?” Nake mũnene ũcio akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Wee anga nĩwaragia Kĩyunani?
English: As Paul was about to be brought into the barracks, he asked the commanding officer, “May I speak to you?” He said, “Do you know Greek?

Kikuyu: Githĩ we tiwe Mũmisiri ũrĩa wambĩrĩirie ngũĩ hĩndĩ ĩmwe, na ũgĩtongoria itoi ngiri inya, mũkiumagara nacio mũgĩthiĩ werũ-inĩ?”
English: Aren’t you then the Egyptian who before these days stirred up to sedition and led out into the wilderness the four thousand men of the Assassins?”

Kikuyu: Paũlũ akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Niĩ ndĩ Mũyahudi wa kuuma Tariso, bũrũri wa Kilikia, na itũũra rĩrĩa ndaciarĩirwo rĩrĩ igweta. Ndagũthaitha, reke njarĩrie andũ aya.”
English: But Paul said, “I am a Jew from Tarsus in Cilicia, a citizen of no insignificant city. I beg you, allow me to speak to the people.”

Kikuyu: Paũlũ aarĩkia kũheo rũũtha nĩ mũnene ũcio wa thigari, akĩrũgama ngathĩ-inĩ agĩkiria kĩrĩndĩ kĩu na moko. Rĩrĩa kĩrĩndĩ gĩakirire, agĩkĩarĩria na rũthiomi rwa Kĩhibirania. Agĩkĩĩra atĩrĩ:
English: When he had given him permission, Paul, standing on the stairs, beckoned with his hand to the people. When there was a great silence, he spoke to them in the Hebrew language, saying,

Kikuyu: “Ariũ a Baba, o na inyuĩ maithe makwa, thikĩrĩriai ngĩĩyarĩrĩria.”
English: “Brothers and fathers, listen to the defense which I now make to you.”

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa maaiguire akĩmaarĩria na rũthiomi rwa Kĩhibirania-rĩ, magĩkira ki. Hĩndĩ ĩyo Paũlũ akiuga atĩrĩ,
English: When they heard that he spoke to them in the Hebrew language, they were even more quiet. He said,

Kikuyu: “Niĩ ndĩ Mũyahudi, wa gũciarĩrwo Tariso itũũra rĩa bũrũri wa Kilikia, no ndaarereirwo itũũra-inĩ rĩĩrĩ. Nĩndathomithirio wega nĩ Gamalieli ũhoro wa watho wa maithe maitũ, na ndaarĩ na kĩyo kĩa maũndũ ma Ngai o ta ũrĩa inyuĩ inyuothe mũtariĩ ũmũthĩ.
English: “I am indeed a Jew, born in Tarsus of Cilicia, but brought up in this city at the feet of Gamaliel, instructed according to the strict tradition of the law of our fathers, being zealous for God, even as you all are today.

Kikuyu: Nĩndanyariiraga arũmĩrĩri a Njĩra ĩno o nginya magakua na nganyiita arũme na andũ-a-nja ngamaikia njeera,
English: I persecuted this Way to the death, binding and delivering into prisons both men and women,

Kikuyu: o ta ũrĩa mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai ũrĩa mũnene na Kĩama gĩothe gĩa athuuri o nao mangĩheana ũira ũcio. O na nĩmaheire marũa ndwarĩre andũ ao kũu Dameski, na niĩ ngĩthiĩ kuo nganyiite andũ acio ndĩmarehe Jerusalemu nĩguo maherithio.
English: as also the high priest and all the council of the elders testify, from whom also I received letters to the brothers, and traveled to Damascus to bring them also who were there to Jerusalem in bonds to be punished.

Kikuyu: “Ta thaa thita ndĩ hakuhĩ gũkinya Dameski-rĩ, o rĩmwe gũkĩgĩa ũtheri mũnene woimire igũrũ, naguo ũkĩnjarĩra mĩena yothe.
English: “As I made my journey and came close to Damascus, about noon suddenly a great light shone around me from the sky.

Kikuyu: Ngĩgwa thĩ, na ngĩigua mũgambo mũnene ũkĩnjarĩria, ũkĩnjĩĩra atĩrĩ, ‘Saũlũ! Saũlũ! Ũũnyariiraga nĩkĩ?’
English: I fell to the ground and heard a voice saying to me, ‘Saul, Saul, why are you persecuting me?’

Kikuyu: “Na niĩ ngĩũria atĩrĩ, ‘Wee nĩ we ũ, Mwathani?’ “Nake akĩnjookeria atĩrĩ, ‘Nĩ niĩ Jesũ wa Nazarethi, ũrĩa ũranyariira.’
English: I answered, ‘Who are you, Lord?’ He said to me, ‘I am Jesus of Nazareth, whom you persecute.’

Kikuyu: Nao andũ arĩa twarĩ nao nĩmoonire ũtheri ũcio, no matiataũkĩirwo nĩ mũgambo wa ũcio wanjaragĩria.
English: “Those who were with me indeed saw the light and were afraid, but they didn’t understand the voice of him who spoke to me.

Kikuyu: “Na niĩ ngĩũria atĩrĩ, ‘Mwathani ngwĩka atĩa?’ “Nake Mwathani akĩnjĩĩra atĩrĩ, ‘Ũkĩra ũtoonye Dameski. Kũu nĩkuo ũrĩĩrĩrwo maũndũ marĩa mothe wathĩrĩirwo gwĩka.’
English: I said, ‘What shall I do, Lord?’ The Lord said to me, ‘Arise, and go into Damascus. There you will be told about all things which are appointed for you to do.’

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, andũ arĩa twarĩ nao makĩĩnyiita guoko makĩndwara Dameski, tondũ ũkengu wa ũtheri ũcio nĩwanduĩte mũtumumu.
English: When I couldn’t see for the glory of that light, being led by the hand of those who were with me, I came into Damascus.

Kikuyu: “Mũndũ wetagwo Anania nĩookire kũnyona, na aarĩ mũndũ weamũrĩire Ngai na ũndũ wa kũrũmia watho, na nĩaheetwo gĩtĩĩo nĩ Ayahudi othe arĩa maatũũraga kũu.
English: “One Ananias, a devout man according to the law, well reported of by all the Jews who lived in Damascus,

Kikuyu: Agĩũka na akĩrũgama hakuhĩ na niĩ akĩnjĩĩra atĩrĩ, ‘Mũrũ wa ithe witũ Saũlũ, cooka kuona!’ Na hĩndĩ o ro ĩyo ngĩhota kũmuona.
English: came to me, and standing by me said to me, ‘Brother Saul, receive your sight!’ In that very hour I looked up at him.

Kikuyu: “Agĩcooka akĩnjĩĩra atĩrĩ, ‘Ngai wa maithe maitũ nĩagũthuurĩte nĩguo ũmenye wendi wake na wone Ũrĩa Mũthingu, o na ũigue ciugo kuuma kanua gake.
English: He said, ‘The God of our fathers has appointed you to know his will, and to see the Righteous One, and to hear a voice from his mouth.

Kikuyu: Nĩũgũtuĩka mũira wake kũrĩ andũ othe wa kũmoimbũrĩra ũrĩa wonete na ũrĩa ũiguĩte.
English: For you will be a witness for him to all men of what you have seen and heard.

Kikuyu: Rĩu nĩ kĩĩ wetereire? Ũkĩra, ũbatithio na ũthambio mehia maku, ũgĩkayagĩra rĩĩtwa rĩake.’
English: Now why do you wait? Arise, be baptized, and wash away your sins, calling on the name of the Lord.’

Kikuyu: “Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa ndaacookire Jerusalemu, ndĩ hekarũ-inĩ ngĩhooya nĩndagĩire na iringi,
English: “When I had returned to Jerusalem and while I prayed in the temple, I fell into a trance

Kikuyu: ngĩkĩona Mwathani akĩaria, nake akĩnjĩĩra atĩrĩ, ‘Ũkĩra narua uume Jerusalemu, tondũ matiigwĩtĩkĩra kum- bũrĩrwo ũhoro wakwa nĩwe.’
English: and saw him saying to me, ‘Hurry and get out of Jerusalem quickly, because they will not receive testimony concerning me from you.’

Kikuyu: “Na niĩ ngĩcookia atĩrĩ, ‘Mwathani, andũ aya nĩmooĩ atĩ ndoimaga thunagogi ĩmwe ngathiĩ ĩrĩa ĩngĩ nĩguo nyiitithie na hũũre andũ arĩa magwĩtĩkĩtie.
English: I said, ‘Lord, they themselves know that I imprisoned and beat in every synagogue those who believed in you.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa Stefano mũira waku aaitirwo thakame, ndaarũngiĩ ho ngĩĩtĩkĩra ũndũ ũcio wĩkwo, na ngaikaria nguo cia andũ arĩa maamũũragire.’
English: When the blood of Stephen, your witness, was shed, I also was standing by, consenting to his death, and guarding the cloaks of those who killed him.’

Kikuyu: “Nake Mwathani akĩnjĩĩra atĩrĩ, ‘Thiĩ, tondũ nĩngũgũtũma kũndũ kũraya kwa andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ.’ ”
English: “He said to me, ‘Depart, for I will send you out far from here to the Gentiles.’ ”

Kikuyu: Kĩrĩndĩ kĩu gĩgĩthikĩrĩria Paũlũ o nginya rĩrĩa oigire ũguo. Hĩndĩ ĩyo gĩkĩaria na mũgambo mũnene kĩanĩrĩire, atĩrĩ, “Mweheriei thĩ! Ndagĩrĩirwo nĩgũtũũra muoyo!”
English: They listened to him until he said that; then they lifted up their voice and said, “Rid the earth of this fellow, for he isn’t fit to live!”

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa kĩanagĩrĩra gĩgĩteaga nguo ciakĩo na gĩkĩũmbũraga rũkũngũ,
English: As they cried out, threw off their cloaks, and threw dust into the air,

Kikuyu: mũnene ũcio wa mbũtũ agĩathana Paũlũ aingĩrio nyũmba ya thigari. Ningĩ agĩathana atĩ Paũlũ ahũũrwo iboko na orio ciũria nĩguo kũmenyeke kĩrĩa gĩatũmaga andũ mamugĩrĩrie ũguo.
English: the commanding officer commanded him to be brought into the barracks, ordering him to be examined by scourging, that he might know for what crime they shouted against him like that.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa maamũkomagia mamũhũũre- rĩ, Paũlũ akĩũria mũnene ũcio wa thigari igana rĩmwe warũngiĩ hau atĩrĩ, “Watho nĩwĩtĩkĩrĩtie mũhũũre raiya wa Roma na kĩboko atanyiitĩkanĩte na ihĩtia?”
English: When they had tied him up with straps, Paul asked the centurion who stood by, “Is it lawful for you to scourge a man who is a Roman, and not found guilty?”

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa mũnene ũcio wa thigari igana rĩmwe aaiguire ũguo, agĩthiĩ kũrĩ mũnene wa mbũtũ, akĩmũhe ũhoro ũcio. Nake akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Rĩu ũgwĩka atĩa? Mũndũ ũyũ nĩ raiya wa Roma.”
English: When the centurion heard it, he went to the commanding officer and told him, “Watch what you are about to do, for this man is a Roman!”

Kikuyu: Mũnene ũcio wa mbũtũ agĩthiĩ kũrĩ Paũlũ, akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Ta njĩĩra atĩrĩ, wee ũrĩ raiya wa Roma?” Nake akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Ĩĩ, ndĩ raiya wa Roma.”
English: The commanding officer came and asked him, “Tell me, are you a Roman?” He said, “Yes.”

Kikuyu: Nake mũnene ũcio wa mbũtũ akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Ndarĩhire thogora mũnene nĩgeetha nduĩke raiya wa Roma.” Nake Paũlũ akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “No niĩ ndaaciarirwo ndĩ raiya wa Roma.”
English: The commanding officer answered, “I bought my citizenship for a great price.” Paul said, “But I was born a Roman.”

Kikuyu: Nao andũ arĩa meendaga kũmũũria ciũria magĩtigana nake o rĩmwe. Mũnene ũcio wa mbũtũ we mwene akĩmaka aamenya atĩ nĩoheete Paũlũ na mĩnyororo, na aarĩ raiya wa Roma.
English: Immediately those who were about to examine him departed from him, and the commanding officer also was afraid when he realized that he was a Roman, because he had bound him.

Kikuyu: Mũthenya ũcio ũngĩ warũmĩrĩire, nĩgũkorwo mũnene ũcio wa mbũtũ nĩeendaga kũmenya wega kĩrĩa Ayahudi maathitangagĩra Paũlũ, akĩmuohora na agĩathana athĩnjĩri-Ngai arĩa anene na athuuri othe a Kĩama magomane. Agĩcooka akĩrehe Paũlũ, na akĩmũrũgamia mbere yao.
English: But on the next day, desiring to know the truth about why he was accused by the Jews, he freed him from the bonds and commanded the chief priests and all the council to come together, and brought Paul down and set him before them.

Kikuyu: Nake Paũlũ agĩkũũrĩra athuuri a Kĩama kĩu maitho akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Ariũ a Baba, nĩndungatĩire Ngai ndĩ na thamiri ĩtarĩ ũcuuke nginya mũthenya wa ũmũthĩ.”
English: Paul, looking steadfastly at the council, said, “Brothers, I have lived before God in all good conscience until today.”

Kikuyu: Nake Anania mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai ũrĩa mũnene, agĩatha andũ arĩa maarũgamĩte hakuhĩ na Paũlũ mamũringe kanua.
English: The high priest, Ananias, commanded those who stood by him to strike him on the mouth.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo Paũlũ akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Wee rũthingo rũrũ rũhakĩtwo mũnyũ mwerũ, we nĩwe ũkũringwo nĩ Ngai! Ũikarĩte hau ũnduĩre ciira kũringana na watho, no wee ũkaregana na watho, ũgaathana atĩ ningwo!”
English: Then Paul said to him, “God will strike you, you whitewashed wall! Do you sit to judge me according to the law, and command me to be struck contrary to the law?”

Kikuyu: Nao andũ arĩa maarũgamĩte hakuhĩ na Paũlũ makĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Anga nĩũkũũmĩrĩria kũruma mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai ũrĩa mũnene?”
English: Those who stood by said, “Do you malign God’s high priest?”

Kikuyu: Nake Paũlũ akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “Ariũ a Baba, ndikũmenyete atĩ ũcio nĩwe mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai ũrĩa mũnene; nĩgũkorwo nĩ kwandĩkĩtwo atĩrĩ: ‘Ndũkanaarie ũũru igũrũ rĩa mũnene wa andũ anyu.’ ”
English: Paul said, “I didn’t know, brothers, that he was high priest. For it is written, ‘You shall not speak evil of a ruler of your people.’ ”*

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, Paũlũ aamenya atĩ amwe ao maarĩ Asadukai, na arĩa angĩ Afarisai, akĩanĩrĩra Kĩama-inĩ kĩu, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Ariũ a Baba, niĩ ndĩ Mũfarisai, na njiarĩtwo nĩ Mũfarisai. Ndĩraciirithio nĩ ũndũ
English: But when Paul perceived that the one part were Sadducees and the other Pharisees, he cried out in the council, “Men and brothers, I am a Pharisee, a son of Pharisees. Concerning the hope and resurrection of the dead I am being judged!”

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa oigire ũguo, gũkĩgĩa na ngarari gatagatĩ ka Afarisai na Asadukai, nakĩo kĩũngano kĩu gĩkĩamũkana.
English: When he had said this, an argument arose between the Pharisees and Sadducees, and the crowd was divided.

Kikuyu: (Asadukai moigaga gũtirĩ ũriũkio, na gũtirĩ araika, o na kana maroho, no Afarisai nĩmetĩkĩtie maũndũ macio mothe.)
English: For the Sadducees say that there is no resurrection, nor angel, nor spirit; but the Pharisees confess all of these.

Kikuyu: Gũkĩgĩa na ngũĩ nene, nao arutani a watho amwe arĩa maarĩ Afarisai makĩrũgama na makĩaria megũmĩire mũno, makiuga atĩrĩ, “Ithuĩ tũtirona ũũru wa mũndũ ũyũ. Ĩ kũngĩkorwo nĩ roho kana mũraika ũmwarĩirie?”
English: A great clamor arose, and some of the scribes of the Pharisees’ part stood up, and contended, saying, “We find no evil in this man. But if a spirit or angel has spoken to him, let’s not fight against God!”

Kikuyu: Nacio ngarari ikĩneneha mũno, o nginya mũnene wa mbũtũ akĩigua guoya nĩkuona ta Paũlũ angĩtarũrangwo tũcunjĩ nĩo. Agĩatha mbũtũ cia thigari ciikũrũke, imweherie harĩo na hinya, na imũtware nyũmba ya thigari.
English: When a great argument arose, the commanding officer, fearing that Paul would be torn in pieces by them, commanded the soldiers to go down and take him by force from among them and bring him into the barracks.

Kikuyu: Ũtukũ ũrĩa warũmĩrĩire, Mwathani akĩrũgama hakuhĩ na Paũlũ, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Wĩyũmĩrĩrie! O ũguo ũheanĩte ũhoro wakwa gũkũ Jerusalemu, noguo ũkaũheana kũu Roma.”
English: The following night, the Lord stood by him and said, “Cheer up, Paul, for as you have testified about me at Jerusalem, so you must testify also at Rome.”

Kikuyu: Rũciinĩ rũrũ rũngĩ, Ayahudi makĩgĩa na ndundu na makĩĩhĩta mwĩhĩtwa matikaarĩe kana manyue matooragĩte Paũlũ.
English: When it was day, some of the Jews banded together and bound themselves under a curse, saying that they would neither eat nor drink until they had killed Paul.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa maathugundĩte ndundu ĩyo maarĩ makĩria ya mĩrongo ĩna.
English: There were more than forty people who had made this conspiracy.

Kikuyu: Magĩthiĩ kũrĩ athĩnjĩri-Ngai arĩa anene na athuuri makĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Ithuĩ nĩtwĩhĩtĩte mwĩhĩtwa wa kwaga kũrĩa kĩndũ kana kũnyua nginya tũũrage Paũlũ.
English: They came to the chief priests and the elders, and said, “We have bound ourselves under a great curse to taste nothing until we have killed Paul.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, inyuĩ mũrĩ hamwe na Kĩama gĩa Athuuri ũriai mũnene wa mbũtũ amũrehe mbere yanyu, mwĩtuĩte ta mũrenda gũtuĩria wega ũhoro wa ciira wake. Ithuĩ nĩtwĩhaarĩirie kũmũũraga atanakinya haha.”
English: Now therefore, you with the council inform the commanding officer that he should bring him down to you tomorrow, as though you were going to judge his case more exactly. We are ready to kill him before he comes near.”

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩa mwanake wa mwarĩ wa nyina na Paũlũ aaiguire ũhoro wa ithugunda rĩu, agĩthiĩ nyũmba ya thigari akĩĩra Paũlũ.
English: But Paul’s sister’s son heard they were lying in wait, and he came and entered into the barracks and told Paul.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo Paũlũ agĩĩta ũmwe wa anene a thigari igana rĩmwe, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Twara mwanake ũyũ kũrĩ mũnene wa mbũtũ; arĩ na ũndũ arenda kũmwĩra.”
English: Paul summoned one of the centurions and said, “Bring this young man to the commanding officer, for he has something to tell him.”

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio mũnene ũcio akĩmuoya, akĩmũtwara kũrĩ mũnene wa mbũtũ. Mũnene ũcio wa thigari igana rĩmwe akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Paũlũ, ũrĩa muohe nĩandũmanĩire anjĩĩra ndeehe mwanake ũyũ kũrĩ we tondũ arĩ na ũndũ arenda gũkwĩra.”
English: So he took him and brought him to the commanding officer and said, “Paul, the prisoner, summoned me and asked me to bring this young man to you. He has something to tell you.”

Kikuyu: Mũnene ũcio wa mbũtũ akĩnyiita mwanake ũcio guoko, akĩmũtwara keeheri-inĩ, akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Nĩ atĩa ũrenda kũnjĩĩra?”
English: The commanding officer took him by the hand, and going aside, asked him privately, “What is it that you have to tell me?”

Kikuyu: Nake mwanake ũcio akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Ayahudi nĩmaiguanĩire makũũrie ũmarehere Paũlũ nĩguo arũgame mbere ya kĩama rũciũ, metuĩte ta marenda gũtuĩria ũhoro wake wega.
English: He said, “The Jews have agreed to ask you to bring Paul down to the council tomorrow, as though intending to inquire somewhat more accurately concerning him.

Kikuyu: Ndũgetĩkĩre ũguo marenda, tondũ andũ makĩria ya mĩrongo ĩna ao mamuoheirie njĩra-inĩ. Nĩmehĩtĩte mwĩhĩtwa wa kwaga kũrĩa kana kũnyua nginya mamũũrage. Na rĩrĩ, nĩmehaarĩirie, metereire wĩtĩkĩre ihooya rĩao.”
English: Therefore don’t yield to them, for more than forty men lie in wait for him, who have bound themselves under a curse to neither eat nor drink until they have killed him. Now they are ready, looking for the promise from you.”

Kikuyu: Nake mũnene ũcio wa mbũtũ akĩĩra mwanake ũcio athiĩ, na akĩmũkaania, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Ndũkeere mũndũ o na ũrĩkũ atĩ nĩũnginyĩirie ũhoro ũcio.”
English: So the commanding officer let the young man go, charging him, “Tell no one that you have revealed these things to me.”

Kikuyu: Mũnene ũcio wa mbũtũ agĩcooka agĩĩta anene aake eerĩ a thigari igana rĩmwe akĩmaatha atĩrĩ, “Haarĩriai gĩkundi gĩa thigari magana meerĩ, na thigari mĩrongo mũgwanja iria ithiiaga ihaicĩte mbarathi, na thigari magana meerĩ iria ithiiaga ikuuĩte matimũ, nĩguo mathiĩ Kaisarea thaa ithatũ cia ũtukũ.
English: He called to himself two of the centurions, and said, “Prepare two hundred soldiers to go as far as Caesarea, with seventy horsemen and two hundred men armed with spears, at the third hour of the night.”†

Kikuyu: Na ningĩ mũhaarĩrie mbarathi cia gũkuua Paũlũ atwarwo kũrĩ Felike ũrĩa barũthi hatarĩ na ũgwati.”
English: He asked them to provide mounts, that they might set Paul on one, and bring him safely to Felix the governor.

Kikuyu: Agĩcooka akĩandĩka marũa ũũ:
English: He wrote a letter like this:

Kikuyu: Nĩ niĩ Kilaudio Lisia, Kũrĩ mũgaathe Barũthi Felike: Nĩndakũgeithia.
English: “Claudius Lysias to the most excellent governor Felix: Greetings.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũyũ aranyiitĩtwo nĩ Ayahudi na mararĩ hakuhĩ kũmũũraga, no ndĩrathiĩ na thigari ciakwa ndĩramũthara, nĩgũkorwo nĩndĩramenyete atĩ nĩ raiya wa Roma.
English: “This man was seized by the Jews, and was about to be killed by them when I came with the soldiers and rescued him, having learned that he was a Roman.

Kikuyu: Nĩndĩrendaga kũmenya kĩrĩa maramũthitangagĩra, na nĩ ũndũ ũcio ndĩramũtwara Kĩama-inĩ kĩao.
English: Desiring to know the cause why they accused him, I brought him down to their council.

Kikuyu: Ndĩrakora atĩ athitangĩirwo maũndũ makoniĩ watho wao, no hatirarĩ ũndũ acuukĩirwo ũngĩratũmire atuĩrwo
English: I found him to be accused about questions of their law, but not to be charged with anything worthy of death or of imprisonment.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa ndĩramenyithirio atĩ kũraarĩ na ithugunda rĩa kũũraga mũndũ ũcio, ndĩramũtũma kũrĩ we o hĩndĩ ĩyo. Ndĩracooka ndĩraatha andũ acio mamũthitangĩte makũrehere thitango ĩrĩa mamũthitangĩire.
English: When I was told that the Jews lay in wait for the man, I sent him to you immediately, charging his accusers also to bring their accusations against him before you. Farewell.”

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio thigari ikĩoya Paũlũ ũtukũ o ta ũrĩa ciaathĩtwo, ikĩmũtwara o nginya Antipatiri.
English: So the soldiers, carrying out their orders, took Paul and brought him by night to Antipatris.

Kikuyu: Naguo mũthenya ũyũ ũngĩ igĩtiga thigari iria ciathiiaga ihaicĩte mbarathi ithiĩ nake, nacio igĩcooka nyũmba ciao.
English: But on the next day they left the horsemen to go with him, and returned to the barracks.

Kikuyu: Thigari icio cia mbarathi ciakinya Kaisarea, ikĩnengera barũthi marũa na ikĩneana Paũlũ kũrĩ we.
English: When they came to Caesarea and delivered the letter to the governor, they also presented Paul to him.

Kikuyu: Nake barũthi aathoma marũa, akĩũria kũrĩa Paũlũ oimĩte. Rĩrĩa aamenyire atĩ oimĩte Kilikia,
English: When the governor had read it, he asked what province he was from. When he understood that he was from Cilicia, he said,

Kikuyu: akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Ngaathikĩrĩria ciira waku andũ arĩa magũthitangĩte mooka.” Agĩcooka agĩathana Paũlũ aikare arangĩrĩtwo kũu nyũmba-inĩ ya ũthamaki ya Herode.
English: “I will hear you fully when your accusers also arrive.” He commanded that he be kept in Herod’s palace.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, thuutha wa mĩthenya ĩtano, Anania, mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai ũrĩa mũnene, agĩikũrũka agĩthiĩ Kaisarea marĩ na athuuri amwe na wakiri wetagwo Teritulo, na makĩrehe thitango ciao cia gũũkĩrĩra Paũlũ kũrĩ barũthi.
English: After five days, the high priest, Ananias, came down with certain elders and an orator, one Tertullus. They informed the governor against Paul.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Paũlũ eetirwo thĩinĩ, Teritulo akĩambĩrĩria kũmũthitanga mbere ya Felike, akiuga atĩrĩ: “Nĩtũkeneire ihinda iraaya rĩa thayũ tũrĩ rungu rwaku, na ũũgĩ waku wa kuona na kabere nĩũgarũrĩte maũndũ marĩa mooru magatuĩka mega bũrũri-inĩ ũyũ.
English: When he was called, Tertullus began to accuse him, saying, “Seeing that by you we enjoy much peace, and that prosperity is coming to this nation by your foresight,

Kikuyu: Kũndũ guothe na maũndũ- inĩ mothe, wee mũgaathe Felike, nĩtwamũkĩrĩte ũndũ ũyũ tũrĩ na ngaatho nene.
English: we accept it in all ways and in all places, most excellent Felix, with all thankfulness.

Kikuyu: No nĩgeetha ndigakũnogie makĩria, nĩngũkũũria na gĩtĩĩo ũtũthikĩrĩrie hanini.
English: But that I don’t delay you, I entreat you to bear with us and hear a few words.

Kikuyu: “Nĩtuonete mũndũ ũyũ arĩ mũndũ wa kũrehe thĩĩna, na nĩarehete ngũĩ gatagatĩ-inĩ ka Ayahudi kũndũ guothe thĩinĩ wa thĩ. We nĩ mũtongoria wa gĩkundi kĩrĩa gĩĩtagwo Anazari,
English: For we have found this man to be a plague, an instigator of insurrections among all the Jews throughout the world,

Kikuyu: na ningĩ nĩageragia gũthaahia hekarũ; nĩ ũndũ ũcio tũkĩmũnyiita. (Na nĩtũngĩamũtuĩrĩire kũringana na watho witũ.
English: He even tried to profane the temple, and we arrested him.†

Kikuyu: No Lisia, mũnene ũcio wa mbũtũ agĩũka akĩmũruta moko-inĩ maitũ na hinya akĩmweheria, na agĩathana athitangi aake moke kũrĩ wee.)
Wee mwene wamũũrangia nĩũkũmenya ma ya ũhoro ũyũ wothe tũmũthitangĩire.”
English: §By examining him yourself you may ascertain all these things of which we accuse him.”

Kikuyu: Nao Ayahudi makĩnyiitanĩra na thitango ĩyo, makiuga atĩ maũndũ macio maarĩ ma ma.
English: The Jews also joined in the attack, affirming that these things were so.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa barũthi aaheneirie Paũlũ na moko aarie-rĩ, Paũlũ oigire atĩrĩ, “Nĩnjũũĩ atĩ nĩũkoretwo ũrĩ mũtui ciira wa rũrĩrĩ rũrũ ihinda rĩa mĩaka mĩingĩ, nĩ ũndũ ũcio nĩngwĩyarĩrĩria ngenete.
English: When the governor had beckoned to him to speak, Paul answered, “Because I know that you have been a judge of this nation for many years, I cheerfully make my defense,

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ mũhũthũ harĩwe kũmenya atĩ ti makĩria ma mĩthenya ikũmi na ĩĩrĩ ĩthirĩte kuuma ndambata Jerusalemu kũhooya Ngai.
English: seeing that you can verify that it is not more than twelve days since I went up to worship at Jerusalem.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa maathitangĩte matianyonire ngĩkararania na mũndũ o na ũrĩkũ hekarũ-inĩ, kana ngĩtũma kĩrĩndĩ kĩgĩe na ngũĩ thunagogi-inĩ, o na kana handũ hangĩ o hothe itũũra-inĩ.
English: In the temple they didn’t find me disputing with anyone or stirring up a crowd, either in the synagogues or in the city.

Kikuyu: Na matingĩhota kuonania ihooto harĩwe cia thitango icio maathitangĩire.
English: Nor can they prove to you the things of which they now accuse me.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, nĩngwĩtĩkĩra atĩ nĩhooyaga Ngai wa maithe maitũ ndĩ mũrũmĩrĩri wa Njĩra ĩyo, o ĩyo metaga ya gĩkundi kĩa nyamũkano. Niĩ nĩnjĩtĩkĩtie maũndũ mothe marĩa megiĩ Watho na marĩa mandĩkĩtwo mabuku-inĩ ma Anabii,
English: But this I confess to you, that according to the Way, which they call a sect, so I serve the God of our fathers, believing all things which are according to the law, and which are written in the prophets;

Kikuyu: na ndĩ na kĩĩrĩgĩrĩro thĩinĩ wa Ngai o ta kĩrĩa andũ aya marĩ nakĩo, atĩ nĩgũgakorwo na kũriũka kwa andũ arĩa athingu na arĩa aaganu.
English: having hope toward God, which these also themselves look for, that there will be a resurrection of the dead, both of the just and unjust.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio nĩndĩĩrutanagĩria hĩndĩ ciothe kũiga thamiri yakwa ĩtarĩ na ũcuuke mbere ya Ngai na mbere ya andũ.
English: In this I also practice always having a conscience void of offense toward God and men.

Kikuyu: “Thuutha wa gũkorwo itarĩ kuo mĩaka mĩingĩ, nĩndokire Jerusalemu nĩgeetha ndeehere andũ akwa iheo nĩ ũndũ wa athĩĩni na ndute maruta nĩ ũndũ wa Ngai.
English: Now after some years, I came to bring gifts for the needy to my nation, and offerings;

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa ndarũmbũyanagia na maũndũ macio nĩmangorire kũu hekarũ-inĩ ndĩtheretie. Gũtiarĩ na gĩkundi kĩa andũ ndaarĩ na kĩo, o na kana ngĩkorwo ngĩruta thĩĩna.
English: amid which certain Jews from Asia found me purified in the temple, not with a mob, nor with turmoil.

Kikuyu: No nĩ kũrĩ Ayahudi a kuuma bũrũri wa Asia arĩa magĩrĩirwo nĩgũkorwo marĩ haha mbere yaku na maathitange angĩkorwo nĩ marĩ ũndũ mangĩnjuukĩra.
English: They ought to have been here before you and to make accusation if they had anything against me.

Kikuyu: Akorwo ti ũguo, aya marĩ haha nĩmagĩrĩirwo moige ihĩtia rĩrĩa maanyonire narĩo rĩrĩa ndaarũgamire mbere ya Kĩama,
English: Or else let these men themselves say what injustice they found in me when I stood before the council,

Kikuyu: tiga ũkorirwo nĩ ũndũ ũyũ ũmwe ndanĩrĩire rĩrĩa ndaarũngiĩ mbere yao ngiuga atĩrĩ: ‘Ũmũthĩ ndũgamĩte mbere yanyu njiirithio nĩ ũndũ nĩnjĩrĩgĩrĩire ũhoro wa kũriũka kwa arĩa akuũ.’ ”
English: unless it is for this one thing that I cried standing among them, ‘Concerning the resurrection of the dead I am being judged before you today!’ ”

Kikuyu: Nake Felike, nĩ ũndũ nĩamenyete wega ũhoro wa Njĩra ĩyo, agĩtĩĩria ciira. Akiuga atĩrĩ, “Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa Lisia, ũcio mũnene wa mbũtũ agooka-rĩ, nĩguo ngaatua ciira waku.”
English: But Felix, having more exact knowledge concerning the Way, deferred them, saying, “When Lysias, the commanding officer, comes down, I will decide your case.”

Kikuyu: Agĩatha mũnene ũcio wa thigari igana rĩmwe aige Paũlũ arĩ mũrangĩre, no amũhe wĩyathi mũnini na etĩkĩrie arata aake marũmbũiye mabata make.
English: He ordered the centurion that Paul should be kept in custody and should have some privileges, and not to forbid any of his friends to serve him or to visit him.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, thuutha wa mĩthenya mĩnini, Felike agĩũka marĩ na mũtumia wake Dirusila, ũrĩa warĩ Mũyahudi mũtumia. Agĩtũmanĩra Paũlũ, na akĩmũthikĩrĩria akĩaria ũhoro wa wĩtĩkio thĩinĩ wa Kristũ Jesũ.
English: After some days, Felix came with Drusilla his wife, who was a Jewess, and sent for Paul and heard him concerning the faith in Christ Jesus.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Paũlũ aaragia ũhoro wa ũthingu, na wa kwĩgirĩrĩria merirĩria ma mwĩrĩ, o na wa ciira ũrĩa ũgooka, Felike akĩigua etigĩra, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Nĩ ũguo! Rĩu no ũthiĩ. Rĩrĩa ndĩrĩgĩa na ihinda rĩagĩrĩire, nĩndĩgũtũmanĩra ũũke.”
English: As he reasoned about righteousness, self-control, and the judgment to come, Felix was terrified, and answered, “Go your way for this time, and when it is convenient for me, I will summon you.”

Kikuyu: Ihinda-inĩ o rĩu no ehokaga atĩ Paũlũ nĩangĩamũheire ihaki, na nĩ ũndũ ũcio nĩaikaraga akĩmũtũmanagĩra, akaaria nake.
English: Meanwhile, he also hoped that money would be given to him by Paul, that he might release him. Therefore also he sent for him more often and talked with him.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa mĩaka ĩĩrĩ yathirire-rĩ, Porikio Fesito agĩcooka ithenya rĩa Felike, no tondũ Felike nĩendaga gũkenia Ayahudi, agĩtiga Paũlũ njeera.
English: But when two years were fulfilled, Felix was succeeded by Porcius Festus, and desiring to gain favor with the Jews, Felix left Paul in bonds.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, mĩthenya ĩtatũ yathira thuutha wa Fesito gũkinya kũu bũrũri-inĩ, akiuma Kaisarea akĩambata agĩthiĩ Jerusalemu.
English: Festus therefore, having come into the province, after three days went up to Jerusalem from Caesarea.

Kikuyu: Nao athĩnjĩri-Ngai arĩa anene na atongoria a Ayahudi magĩũka harĩ we, makĩmwĩra maũndũ marĩa Paũlũ aathitangĩirwo.
English: Then the high priest and the principal men of the Jews informed him against Paul, and they begged him,

Kikuyu: Nĩmahĩkire gũthaitha Fesito atĩ nĩ ũndũ wa wega wake kũrĩ o etĩkĩre Paũlũ atwarwo Jerusalemu, nĩgũkorwo nĩmahaaragĩria kũmuoheria njĩra-inĩ mamũũrage.
English: asking a favor against him, that he would summon him to Jerusalem, plotting to kill him on the way.

Kikuyu: Nake Fesito akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “Paũlũ ahingĩirwo Kaisarea, na niĩ nĩngũcooka kuo o narua.
English: However Festus answered that Paul should be kept in custody at Caesarea, and that he himself was about to depart shortly.

Kikuyu: Tondũ ũcio no tũthiĩ na atongoria amwe anyu nĩgeetha mathiĩ magathitangĩre mũndũ ũcio kuo, angĩkorwo nĩ harĩ ũndũ mũũru ekĩte.”
English: “Let them therefore”, he said, “that are in power among you go down with me, and if there is anything wrong in the man, let them accuse him.”
